_meta.description: Room templates for the premonition level (Level 0). The procedural generator selects a shell based on the disaster's tags, then applies per-tag overrides to create location-specific rooms. Room keys are generic (staging_area, approach, etc.) — the override provides the final room name.
_meta.usage: game_logic._build_premonition_rooms(disaster_data) reads this file, selects a shell, resolves overrides, and returns a rooms dict compatible with current_level_rooms_world_state.

npc_archetypes_premonition._note: These are ARCHETYPE templates, not the named NPCs in npcs.json. At level init, _build_premonition_rooms reads the current shell name and picks the matching dialogue variant for each archetype. The archetype's 'name' is randomized from a pool at runtime.

visionary.description_template: A [color=#5eafd2]{age} {appearance} [/color] who keeps looking around nervously, visibly agitated. Something is clearly wrong.
appearance_pool: person with headphones around their neck

warning.text:
'I'm sorry for freaking out but something terrible is about to happen. 
Please, [color=#b266ff]I can't explain how but I SAW it[/color]. You have to help me warn the others — nobody will listen to me.'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]Look, I believe you, but you need to calm tf down. I'll help warn people.[/color]
[color=ffff00]You need to calm down. What exactly did you see?[/color]
[color=ff4444][Walk away][/color]

grateful.text:
'Thank you. THANK YOU.
Maybe I'm wrong, but [color=#3399ff]the others should be warned[/color]. People in charge are saying I need to vacate.
[color=#cc9900]I'll meet you outside.[/color]'

explaining.text:
'I saw us all die, the whole thing play out like I was right there in the moment. [color=FF3131]{visionary_explains}[/color]

I know how it sounds. But we have to go. NOW.'

options.text: [color=#3399ff]Okay. Let's get people out of here.[/color]
[color=#ffff00]I think you're having a panic attack. Just breathe.[/color]
[color=#ff4444]It's just a panic attack. Sit down. I'll get you some water. We aren't going anywhere.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: They look at you, trembling. The panic in their eyes wavers, replaced by a deep, terrifying doubt. '[color=#ff99cc]You... you really think it was just my imagination?[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: They stare at you in absolute horror, realizing you are going to let them die. '[color=#ff0000]You're crazy! I'M LEAVING![/color]'

rebelled_and_ran.text:
'[color=#ff0000]I'm not dying here because you're too stubborn to listen![/color]'

They shove past you and sprint blindly for the exit, leaving you behind in the crowd.

convinced_to_stay.text:
They look at you, trembling, but slowly sit back down. '[color=#ff99cc]You... you really think it was just my imagination?[/color]

Maybe you're right. Maybe I'm just overreacting. I'm sorry for causing a scene.'

frustrated.text:
'[color=#ffa500]I'm NOT panicking! I'm trying to SAVE — fine. Fine. Go.[/color]
[color=#ff00ff]But when it happens, remember I tried.[/color]'

abandoned.text: '[color=#ff4444]Wait — where are you going? Please! PLEASE![/color]'

skeptic.description_template: A[color=#5086ce] {appearance} [/color]who seems more annoyed than concerned. They keep rolling their eyes at [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color].

greeting.text:
They look at you like you've lost your mind. To be fair, from where they're standing, you have.

Okay. You need to calm down. Whatever you think is happening — "

options.text: "I know exactly what's happening. Follow me."
"I can't explain it. I just know. Please."

confrontational.text:
Look, you're scaring people. I don't know what's going on with you, but you need to stop."

They're not going to move unless you give them something concrete.

options.text: "Something is going to collapse/crash/ignite in the next few minutes. I've seen it."
"Fine. Don't listen. But when it happens, run."

reluctant_listener.text:
Something in your voice catches them. They're not convinced. But they're not walking away either.

"...How do you know?"

options.text: "I don't have time to explain. Move."
"I've seen things before. I've always been right."

convinced.text:
They're not happy about it. But they're moving.

"This better not be a joke."They're not happy about it. But they're moving.

This better not be a joke."

abandoned.text: They shake their head and walk back into the crowd. You've lost them.

dismissive.text: '[color=#ff0000]Oh great, another one. Let me guess — the sky is falling?[/color] Get bent. I've got things to do.'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]I think we should listen to them. They seem really scared, and if they're right..[/color]
[color=#ff00ff]Other people are already leaving. You should too.[/color]
[color=#ffa500]Fine, stay. It's your funeral.[/color]
[color=#ff0000][Walk away][/color]

considering.text: '...their commitment to the bit is giving, but there's probably an explanation. There's ALWAYS an explanation for shit like this.'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]What if this time there isn't? What do you lose by leaving?[/color]
[color=#ff00ff]I can't force you. But I'm leaving, and I hope you do too.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message:
'[color=#3399ff]Other people are leaving? ...how many?[/color]'

You gesture to the panicked crowd.

'[color=#00ff00]...Fine. FINE. But if this is nothing, you owe me a drink.[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message:
'[color=#3399ff]Other people are leaving? ...how many?[/color]'

You point to the crowd, but they aren't having it.

'[color=#ff0000]Nice try. I'm not a sheep.[/color]
[color=#ff0000]Go follow your paranoid friend or whoever that is.[/color]'

persuasion_check.success_message:
'[color=#00ff00]...Shit. Okay. Okay, let's go.[/color]
[color=#00ff00]But I'm coming back for my stuff later.[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message:
'[color=#ff0000]You almost had me. ALMOST.[/color]
[color=#ff0000]But no. I'm staying right here.[/color]'

grudging.text: '[color=#ff00ff]Gross. I can't believe I'm doing this. Lead the way, I guess.[/color]'

stubborn.text: '[color=#ffa500]I appreciate the concern, really. But I'm not going anywhere.[/color]'

hostile.text: '[color=#ff0000]Get the fuck away from me. You and that lunatic are causing a scene.[/color]'

friend.description_template: Someone who seems to know [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color]. They look torn — wanting to support their friend but clearly uncomfortable.

greeting.text: They can tell something is wrong immediately. Hey. Hey — what's happening? You look like you've seen a ghost."

options.text: "Something is about to go very wrong here. Trust me."
"We need to get everyone out. Now. I'll explain later."

trusting.text:
They don't ask questions. That's the thing about people who actually care about you — they can tell the difference between crazy and scared.

"Okay. What do you need me to do?"

uncertain.text: '[color=#ffff00]Look, I don't know what's going on, but[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]has never acted like this before. Maybe we should just... step away for a bit? Just to be safe?[/color]'

convinced.text:
'[color=#00ff00]I'm staying with [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color]. Whatever happens, I can't just let them leave alone.

If they say we should go, I'm going. You do what you want.[/color]'

panicking.description_template: A {role_title} who is pacing erratically. Their eyes are wide with terror, and they are hyperventilating.

greeting.text:
They're already half-convinced something is wrong. They just needed someone else to say it first.

I knew it. I KNEW something felt off. What do we do?"

options.text: "Follow me. Exit. Now."

following.text: "Okay. Okay, I'm coming. Lead the way."

hysterical.text: 'We need to get out! I can't breathe...[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]freaked me the fuck out! We're all going to die!'

options.text: Hey, look at me. Breathe. We are going to walk out of here together right now.
You're right, it's not safe! Run for the exit!

persuasion_check.success_message: They take a shaky breath and nod. 'Okay... okay, lead the way.'
persuasion_check.failure_message: They rip their arm away from you. 'DON'T TOUCH ME! GET AWAY!'

calmed_down.text: 'I'm right behind you. Please don't leave me.'

hyperventilating.text: 'I can't... I can't move... my legs won't work...'

blind_panic.text: They scream and sprint blindly down the hall, tripping over their own feet. You have no idea where they went.

fatalist.description_template: A {role_title} minding their own business from afar. They saw the whole commotion and clearly decided it's not worth worrying about.

resigned.text: They watch you panicking with a sort of morbid curiosity. '[color=#ffa500]You look like you know something we don't. Have you ever considered: whatever happens, happens?[/color]'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]It doesn't have to happen to YOU, dipshit! Move![/color]
[color=#ffa500]I'm not leaving you here to die. Get up.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: They stare at you for a long second, weighing your conviction. '[color=#00ff00]...Fine. Let's see if you're right.[/color]' They stand up.
persuasion_check.failure_message:
'[color=#ff1]'Nah. I've had a good life.'
They see the dumbfounded look on your face and almost seem amused. '[color=#ff0000]You go ahead, though.[/color]'

given_up.text: '[color=#ff0000]They close their eyes and lean their head back. They don't respond anymore.[/color]'

resigned.text: '[color=#ffa500]Oh come ON, are you kidding me? Even if it IS true, if it's our time, it's our time. You gonna listen to that fuckin psycho?[/color]'

options.text: [color=00ff00]It's not our time. We have a warning. We can do something about it.[/color]
[color=ff0000]Whatever. Sit here and die if you want.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: '[color=#00ff00]Jesus, I was just kidding but you're really serious, aren't you?[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message:
'They are clearly over it. [color=#ff0000]OH MY GOD BORINNNG.
Go on without me, champ, save yourself, huh?
[/color]Now they're openly mocking you.
[color=#ff0000]Oh no, now I'm gonna die, wah wah. Fucking idiot.[/color]'

spark_of_hope.text: '[color=#00ff00]If you really think something is going on... I'll follow you. For now.[/color]'

given_up.text: '[color=#ff0000]They close their eyes and lean their head back. They don't respond anymore.[/color]'

distracted.description_template: A {role_title} with oversized headphones on, completely absorbed in their phone screen.

oblivious.text: They don't even notice you until you are standing directly in their personal space. They pull out one earbud. '[color=#ffa500]...What? Did you say something?[/color]'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]*Yank the headphones out*[/color] THERE IS NO TIME! RUN!
[color=#ffa500]Look around you! You have to leave right now![/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: They drop their phone, the spell finally broken by your panic. '[color=#00ff00]Oh shit! Okay, I'm out![/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff0000]What is your problem?![/color]' They snatch their headphones back.

ignored.text: They roll their eyes, jam the earbud back in, and turn up the volume.

oblivious.text: '[color=#ffa500]...Hold on, let me finish this level. What did you say? Evacuate?[/color]'

options.text: [color=00ff00]*Yank the headphones off*[/color] THERE IS NO TIME. RUN!
[color=d3d3d3]Hey, please, it's really dangerous here. You need to leave.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: '[color=#00ff00]They drop their phone in shock. 'Whoa, okay, geez! I'm moving!'[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff0000]They snatch their headphones back. 'Don't touch my stuff, weirdo!'[/color]'

persuasion_check.success_message: '[color=#ffff00]For some reason, they actually listen to you.[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff0000]They snatch their headphones back. 'Don't touch my stuff, weirdo!'[/color]'

snapped_to_reality.text: '[color=#00ff00]Okay, okay, I'm right behind you! Just go![/color]'

ignored.text: '[color=#ff0000]They put their headphones back on and turn their back to you, swiping on their screen.[/color]'

selfish.description_template: An aggressive {role_title} clutching their belongings tightly. They look ready to push past anyone to save themselves.

hostile.text: They glare at you as you approach, gripping their belongings. '[color=#ff0000]Whatever you're selling, I don't want it. Back off.[/color]'

options.text: [color=#00ff00]I'm not selling anything. I'm trying to save your life. Move![/color]
[color=#ffa500]Get out of the way before you get yourself killed.[/color]

persuasion_check.success_message: They look around, see the raw terror in your eyes, and their survival instinct overrides their attitude. '[color=#00ff00]Out of my way![/color]' they yell, shoving past you toward the exit.
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff0000]Yeah, right. Nice try. I'm not giving up my spot.[/color]'

dismissive.text: '[color=#ff0000]Don't you have someone else to bother?[/color]' They turn their back on you.

hostile.text: '[color=#ff0000]Back off! I'm not waiting around for whatever disaster they're blabbering about. I'm looking out for number one![/color]'

options.text: Fine, go alone!
Hey, relax! We can all get out if we work together.

persuasion_check.success_message:
'[color=#00ff00]They narrow their eyes. 'Fine, I'll come with.
But if you're wrong, you and I are gonna talk.'[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff0000]They shove past you. 'I don't need your help, loser!'[/color]'

using_you.text: '[color=#00ff00]Move it! And stay out of my way![/color]'

authority_figure.description_template:
A {role_title} who looks like they've been having a long day. They're watching[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]with a mix of concern and irritation.

'Gotttta be fucking kidding me.' they say to themselves.

greeting.text:
They've seen everything. They're not impressed by vague warnings.

"What's the nature of the threat? Be specific."

options.text: "Structural failure / vehicle / fire — imminent. Evacuate now."
"I can't prove it. I just know."

evaluating.text:
They study you for a long moment. They've learned to read people who are lying versus people who are terrified.

"I'm going to take this seriously for thirty seconds. Show me."

options.text: [Lead them toward the danger zone to show them the specific hazard]

convinced.text:
They're already on their radio.

"All units, possible emergency — initiate precautionary evacuation protocol. Now."

They glance at you. "Let's hope you're wrong."

dismissing.text:
"Move along. If you see something specific, come back and tell me."

They're not going to help.

professional.text:
'[color=#3399ff]Whoa there. Are you with the person causing a disturbance?
Is there a problem here? I just received a few complaints.[/color]'

options.text: '[color=#ffff00]There's a serious safety concern. People need to evacuate.[/color]'
'[color=#ffa500]I think something bad is about to happen. I know that sounds crazy.[/color]'
'[color=#00ffff]No problem, officer. Just a misunderstanding.[/color]'

skeptical_authority.text:
'[color=#ffff00]A safety concern?
*Their hand instinctively goes to their radio.*
What kind of concern? I need specifics.[/color]'

options.text:
[color=0000ff]*Tell the truth*[/color] —
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] started panicking out of nowhere that something bad is going to happen, but you don't have concrete evidence.
It's a long shot, but maybe they'll take it seriously.

'[color=#ffff00]*Bend the truth a bit*[/color] —
'Oh yeah? Well 
-you should know multiple people have also reported feeling unsafe. To me for some reason. And now I'm telling you because it's your duty to take it seriously.'

[color=ff0000]*Cause a massive scene*[/color] —
Punch them in the face, scream at the top of your lungs and maybe find a table to flip.
Raw panic is the only way to get people moving right now.
'[color=#00ffff]Never mind. Forget I said anything.[/color]'

persuasion_check.success_message:
'[color=#00ff00]...Alright. I'm calling it in.
Everyone needs to move to the exits calmly.[/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message: '[color=#ff00ff]I can't evacuate based on a hunch. I need concrete evidence of danger.[/color]'

persuasion_check.success_message:
'[color=#00ff00]You throw your arms up, scream at the top of your lungs, and violently shove {authority_figure}. 'WE'RE ALL GOING TO DIE! RUN!'

Panic is a virus, and you just released it. Someone shrieks, and suddenly the crowd shatters into absolute chaos, stampeding for the exits! It's a total mess, but they are leaving![/color]'
persuasion_check.failure_message:
'[color=#ff00ff]You throw your arms up, scream at the top of your lungs, and violently shove {authority_figure}. 'WE'RE ALL GOING TO DIE! RUN!'[/color]

Instead of stampeding for the exits... [color=#3399ff]everyone stops. They stare at you in stunned silence. {authority_figure} freezes, reaching for their cuffs, completely distracted.[/color] The silence stretches on for a second too long.

...and just like that, [color=#ff0000]you've doomed everyone.[/color]'

commotion_death_triggered.text: The silence stretches on for a second too long... [color=#ff0000]You've doomed everyone.[/color]

evacuating.text:
'...Alright. I'm calling it in.
[color=#ff00ff]ATTENTION EVERYONE. We're conducting a precautionary evacuation. Please move to the nearest exit in an orderly fashion.[/color]'

refused.text:
'[color=#ff00ff]I understand your concern, but I can't act without evidence.
If you see something specific, come find me.[/color]'

dismissed.text:
'[color=#ffa500]Alright. You've had your fun.
Keep it down, please.[/color]'

npc_archetypes._note: These are ROLE slots, not specific NPCs. The cast generator assigns named NPCs to each role. The visionary is always present. Other roles are filled from the cast pool.

terminal._description: Linear flow: arrival → waiting → boarding → platform/view. Covers airports, train stations, ferry docks, bus terminals.

base.name: Arrival Hall
base.description: A large, busy hall filled with people. Announcements echo from overhead speakers. The air smells of fast food and floor cleaner.
base.first_entry_text:
Most people seem shaken up after[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]freaked out, but no one is actually *doing* anything about it.
Are you going to just stand here?

objects.name: overhead speakers
objects.description: Speakers overhead crackle with muzak and announcements.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
The departure lounge is bright and efficient and it smells like coffee and jet fuel.

You've been standing here for two minutes trying to convince yourself that what you know isn't real.

It's real. [color=ff4444]Get people off this concourse.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The main terminal entrance. Announcements play overhead. None of them mention what you already know is going to happen.

transportation_air+mechanical_failure.name: Airport Terminal — Concourse B
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.description: A sprawling airport concourse stretches out beneath harsh fluorescent light. Travelers drag luggage past overpriced shops. Flight numbers shuffle on the overhead monitors. Gate B7 is to the north.
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You were next in line to hand over your boarding pass when a {age} {appearance}—{visionary}—started screaming about an explosion.

Security tackled them, the line stopped, and now you're stuck waiting while they sort it out. You're definitely missing your flight.

examine_details.departures_board: A massive LCD display. Your flight — FL-180 — now reads ON TIME. Gate B7.
examine_details.security_checkpoint: A bored TSA agent waves people through the scanner.
examine_details.duty_free_window: Perfume, liquor, and oversized Toblerones. The usual.

transportation_rail+impact_blunt.name: {city_name} Central Station — Main Hall
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.description: The grand hall of {city_name} Central Station echoes with footsteps and announcements. A massive clock hangs from the vaulted ceiling. The northbound platform is to the north.
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.first_entry_text:
You were jogging toward Platform 3 when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]collided with you, grabbing your jacket and screaming that the trains were going to crash.

By the time you pushed them off, the 7:15 commuter express doors had already closed. You're stuck here.

examine_details.arrivals_board: Track 3 - 7:15 Commuter Express - ON TIME. That's yours.
examine_details.station_clock: A grand analog clock. The second hand ticks with mechanical precision.
examine_details.platform_sign: PLATFORM 3 — Northbound Express. An arrow points north.

transportation_water+drowning_water.name: Lake Serenity Ferry Terminal
transportation_water+drowning_water.description: A small waterfront terminal with wooden benches and the smell of lake water. The 'Queen Isabella' sits at the dock, engines idling. Boarding is to the north.
transportation_water+drowning_water.first_entry_text:
You had your ticket out. You were walking to the dock.

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started shrieking about drowning and fire, blocking the main double doors. The terminal guards are trying to restrain them, but the boarding window is closing fast.

examine_details.ferry_schedule: Queen Isabella — 3:00 PM departure. Lake Serenity to Port Carroll. 45 minutes.
examine_details.life_jacket_station: A bright orange cabinet labeled LIFE JACKETS. It looks like it hasn't been opened in years.
examine_details.dock_view: The ferry sits low in the water, rocking gently. Paint is peeling from the hull.

base.name: Waiting Area
base.description: Rows of seats fill a bland waiting area. A TV mounted on the wall plays the news on low volume. People check their phones, stare at nothing, or sleep uncomfortably.

objects.name: wall-mounted TV
objects.description: A battered wall-mounted TV sits here. Its display is dark and silent.

transportation_air+mechanical_failure.name: Gate B7 — Boarding Area
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.description: Blue fabric seats face floor-to-ceiling windows. Beyond the glass, your plane sits on the tarmac. A ground crew member kicks a tire, then walks away. The gate agent types something into her terminal.
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You drop into a seat near the window. The plane is right there — close enough to read the tail number. It looks fine. Of course it looks fine.

A few other passengers are already waiting. One of them is staring at the plane like it owes them money.

examine_details.gate_window: Through the glass, Flight 801 sits on the tarmac. The ground crew finishes loading luggage. Nothing looks wrong. You're not sure why you expected it to.
examine_details.boarding_pass_scanner: The gate agent's scanner. A small screen reads BOARDING SOON.
examine_details.overhead_monitor: FL-180 — BOARDING IN 10 MINUTES. The words blink green.

transportation_rail+impact_blunt.name: Platform 3 — Waiting Benches
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.description: A concrete platform with metal benches. The tracks gleam in the morning light. A digital sign counts down: NEXT TRAIN — 4 MIN.
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.first_entry_text:
You settle onto the cold metal bench. The platform is getting crowded — the morning rush.

Someone near the edge keeps looking down at the tracks. They seem agitated.

examine_details.platform_edge: A yellow safety line painted on the concrete. The drop to the tracks is about four feet.
examine_details.countdown_display: NEXT TRAIN — 4 MIN. The numbers tick down.
examine_details.safety_line: DO NOT CROSS. The paint is worn from years of foot traffic.

transportation_water+drowning_water.name: Boarding Dock — Waiting Pier
transportation_water+drowning_water.description: A weathered wooden pier with rope railings. The Queen Isabella's gangplank is being lowered. Seagulls circle overhead, screaming.
transportation_water+drowning_water.first_entry_text:
You lean against the railing. The lake smells like iron and algae. The ferry crew is shouting to each other as they lower the gangplank.

A teenager near you is muttering something and won't stop fidgeting.

examine_details.gangplank: A metal ramp being lowered from the ferry to the dock. It clangs against the pier.
examine_details.rope_railing: Thick nautical rope strung between wooden posts. It sways in the breeze.
examine_details.lake_surface: The lake is calm. Almost too calm. A dark shape moves beneath the surface — just a fish.

base.name: Concessions
base.description: A small food court area. The smell of burnt coffee and microwaved pastries hangs in the air. A bored attendant stares at their phone.

transportation_air+mechanical_failure.name: Airport Terminal Coffee Shop
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.description: A cramped Aerobean franchise wedged between gates. The barista looks like she hasn't slept in two days. The coffee smells burned.

examine_details.abandoned_newspaper: Today's McKinley Herald. The headline reads: FAA GROUNDS FLEET FOR INSPECTION — 'ROUTINE,' SAYS SPOKESPERSON. The article is circled in red pen. Someone wrote 'BULLSHIT' in the margin.

transportation_rail+impact_blunt.name: Platform Kiosk
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.description: A small newsstand and coffee window. The vendor is arguing with someone about correct change.

examine_details.today_newspaper: McKinley Herald. A small article on page 3: RAIL UNION WARNS OF 'DEFERRED MAINTENANCE CRISIS.' Nobody seems to be reading it.
examine_details.commuter_schedule: A laminated schedule. Several trains have been cancelled this week. Mechanical issues.

transportation_water+drowning_water.name: Dockside Snack Bar
transportation_water+drowning_water.description: A weathered wooden shack selling fried fish, lemonade, and sunscreen. A hand-painted sign reads LAST FOOD BEFORE PORT CARROLL.

examine_details.coast_guard_notice: COAST GUARD ADVISORY: Small craft warning in effect until 6 PM. The ferry isn't a small craft... right?
examine_details.weather_radio: A dusty radio crackling with static. You catch fragments: '...pressure dropping... advisory extended...'

base.name: Boarding Zone
base.description: The point of no return. People shuffle forward in a line, documents in hand. Beyond here, you're committed.
base.first_entry_text:
This is it. Once you step through, you're on board.

Something feels wrong. You can't explain it.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
Gates A through F. Hundreds of people waiting for flights they won't all make it onto.

You know which gate. You know the time. You need to move people away from that side of the building [color=ff4444]right now.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The main concourse. The volume of the crowd makes it hard to think. You're trying very hard not to look at gate F.

transportation_air+mechanical_failure.name: Gate B7 — Jet Bridge
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.description: The narrow jet bridge stretches from the gate to the plane's door. The carpet is stained. The walls flex slightly as you walk. Through the small windows, you can see the wing.
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
The gate agent scans boarding passes. The line moves forward.

You glance out the jet bridge window. The wing. Something about the wing.

Your stomach drops. Not turbulence. Not nerves. Something *wrong*.

examine_details.jet_bridge_window: Through the scratched plexiglass, you can see the wing of Flight 801. The rivets along the leading edge catch the light. One of them — is that loose? No. You're imagining things. ...Aren't you?
examine_details.plane_door: The open door of the aircraft. A flight attendant smiles mechanically from inside.

transportation_rail+impact_blunt.name: Platform 3 — Boarding Point
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.description: The train pulls in with a screech of brakes. Doors hiss open. The crowd surges forward.
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.first_entry_text:
The train arrives. Everyone moves at once.

You step toward the doors, but something makes you hesitate. A sound — was that the brakes? Or something grinding beneath the carriage?

examine_details.train_doors: The doors stand open, light spilling from inside. The gap between platform and train is wider than usual.
examine_details.gap_warning: MIND THE GAP. The words are almost worn away.
examine_details.carriage_window: Through the window, you can see the other side of the tracks. Another train sits idle — its wheels are being inspected by a maintenance crew. They look worried.

transportation_water+drowning_water.name: Gangplank — Boarding
transportation_water+drowning_water.description: The metal gangplank sways slightly underfoot. Crew members in orange vests wave passengers aboard. The ferry groans as it shifts against the dock.
transportation_water+drowning_water.first_entry_text:
You step onto the gangplank. It flexes under your weight.

The ferry rocks. A crew member grabs the railing. 'Just the wake from a speedboat,' he says. He doesn't look convinced.

examine_details.gangplank_railing: Cold metal. Your knuckles are white around it.
examine_details.hull_waterline: The ferry sits lower in the water than the painted line suggests it should.
examine_details.crew_member: A deckhand in an orange vest. He keeps checking his watch and looking at the sky.

base.name: Observation Point
base.description: A spot with a view of the departure zone. From here, you can see everything — the vehicle, the people, the moment it all goes wrong.
base.first_entry_text:
From here, you can see it all.

And then it happens.

transportation_air+mechanical_failure.name: Terminal Window — Runway View
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.description: The massive terminal window frames the runway. Flight 801 taxis toward the threshold. The engines spool up.
transportation_air+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You're watching through the terminal glass, heart hammering. Flight 801 is accelerating down the strip without you, all because you got swept up in the frantic screaming[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started at the gate.

The nose lifts, engines roar, gear begins to retract —and then you see it. The left wing begins flexing, [color=cc9900]wobbling up and down at an impossible angles.[/color] The seam connecting the wing slowly unzips from the back like a [color=ff4444]jagged wound[/color], and the outer wing-tip simply snaps away.

[color=ffff00]But it doesn't end there.[/color] The sudden imbalance forces the engine to scream —the wing slamming up and down due to the force of the engine turbines— [color=b266ff]before it shears off[/color] most of [color=b266ff]its pylons and is sucked upward[/color]. The front seam of the wing is secured a little too well, and the wing arcs [color=b266ff]back into the plane's cabin ceiling, engine-first[/color], punching through the roof like a massive, spinning projectile. 

The fuselage erupts in a spray of [color=#ffff00]insulation[/color], [color=#ff4444]blood[/color] and [color=#ffb366]fire[/color]. The aircraft rolls, [color=#ffb366]carving a burning arc into the tarmac[/color], and the sound hits the glass a half-second later.
A single, absolute [color=#ff4444]BOOM[/color].

[color=#00ccff]{reaction}[/color] Just like[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]said.
You were supposed to be in one of those seats.

examine_details.runway_view: Emergency vehicles converge from the perimeter roads. The fireball is still rising. The wreckage is scattered across three runways.
examine_details.terminal_glass: A hairline crack runs down the pane from the shockwave. You press your palm to it. It's warm.

transportation_rail+impact_blunt.name: Platform End — Track View
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.description: The far end of the platform. You shouldn't be here. Staff only. From here, you can see the tracks curving away under the river bridge.
transportation_rail+impact_blunt.first_entry_text:
You didn't board. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] grabbed your jacket with a white-knuckled grip, screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the sound of grinding teeth'[/color] as they pulled you back from the yellow line. You retreated to the far end of the platform, leaning against the cold tile to catch your breath.

The commuter train pulls away, humming as it picks up speed into the tunnel. You notice the signal light flicker and turn [color=#00ff00]green[/color]—but the switch hasn't fully seated. You see the track geometry ahead, a jagged, [color=#FF3131]wrong-angled silhouette[/color] in the dim tunnel lights. 

Then comes the [color=#aaaaaa]shriek of tortured iron[/color]. 

A freight train, massive and unrelenting, rounds the curve at full speed. It doesn't just hit the passenger cars; it [color=#FF3131]erases[/color] them. The collision is a [color=#FF3131]bass-heavy thud[/color] that you feel in your marrow before the sound even reaches your ears. The carriages [color=#aaaaaa]accordion[/color] into one another, steel beams folding like wet cardboard as the kinetic energy is hammered through every seat and passenger.

The platform shakes with the force of a localized earthquake, slamming you hard against the wall. Dust and [color=#FF3131]hot copper smells[/color] billow out of the tunnel mouth in a dry, choking cloud.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] You're shaking. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] wasn't crazy. They saw the exact moment the steel would fail. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]compacted[/color] in that lead car.

examine_details.track_view: The wreckage blocks both tracks. One carriage has rolled entirely off the bridge and hangs suspended by its coupling. Emergency sirens layer over each other from every direction.
examine_details.emergency_phone: The red emergency phone is ringing. You pick it up. The line is dead.

transportation_water+drowning_water.name: Harbour Wall — East Berth
transportation_water+drowning_water.description: The upper section of the harbour wall. You came up here for the view. From here, you can see the full breadth of the channel.
transportation_water+drowning_water.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] wouldn't let you board. They clawed at your arm, screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the metal screaming under the water'[/color] until the dockmaster finally physically shoved you both back. You’re standing on the harbor wall, heart pounding, watching the ship pull away into the dark.

The cruise liner is a floating jewel box, lit up like a small city. You can hear the distant, tinny thump of a bassline and the faint sound of people laughing on the lido deck.

Then you see it. A speedboat, running completely dark, carving a [color=#FF3131]white-knuckle wake[/color] through the black water. It’s moving too fast. It isn't turning.

It slams into the hull [color=#FF3131]low and deep[/color]. The sound isn't an explosion—it’s the sound of a [color=#aaaaaa]thousand rusted bones snapping at once[/color]. A sickening, structural groan that vibrates through the harbor wall and into your teeth.

The speedboat’s fuel tank ruptures, painting the side of the liner in [color=#FF3131]clinging, liquid fire[/color]. Through the portholes, you see figures pressed against the glass as the ocean begins to [color=#55aaff]gulp[/color] into the breach. The massive ship groans, tilting with a slow, ponderous inevitability before the stabilizers give way with a [color=#FF3131]thunderous crack[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] knew. They knew the exact sound the hull would make when it unzipped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]shredded[/color] in that cabin.

examine_details.harbour_panorama: The ship is tilting past the point of no return. Lifeboats are dropping, but not enough of them. The water between the hull and the harbour wall is full of people.
examine_details.dock_railing: You grip the railing until your hands hurt. Someone beside you is on the phone with emergency services. They keep saying the same address over and over.

visionary_player+transportation_air+mech.first_entry_text:
You couldn't make yourself board.

The images had been playing since you woke up — [color=#FF3131]the wing, the fireball, the screaming metal[/color] — and your legs simply refused to carry you down the jetway.

You stood at the gate, blocking people, making a scene, until the attendant physically asked you to step aside. You backed away from the door. You watched Flight 801 push back from the gate.

You're pressing your hands against the terminal glass when [color=#FF3131]the nose lifts[/color] — and then you see what you already knew was there.

visionary_player+transportation_rail+imp.first_entry_text:
You grabbed the person next to you at the platform edge.

You don't know why. You just knew — the way you know things sometimes, the way you've always known things, the way nobody has ever believed — that [color=#FF3131]the next train was not going to stop.[/color]

They called you insane. The platform guard threatened to remove you. You kept moving people back anyway, physically, with your hands, until there was nobody near the yellow line.

The sound of grinding teeth starts somewhere in the tunnel. Then it gets [color=#FF3131]much louder.[/color]

visionary_player+transportation_water+dr.first_entry_text:
You wouldn't let anyone board.

The dockmaster called it a disruption. The other passengers called it worse. You kept talking about [color=#FF3131]'the metal screaming under the water'[/color] — words you couldn't explain, images you'd had since the night before — until they physically removed you from the gangway.

You're standing on the harbor wall now. The ship is pulling away from the dock, full of passengers who didn't listen.

The hull makes a sound. [color=#FF3131]That sound.[/color]

You already know what happens next.

venue._description: Hub-and-spoke: entrance → main floor → side areas → exterior/overlook. Covers nightclubs, theaters, malls, carnivals, plazas, escalator incidents.

base.name: Entrance
base.description: The main entrance to the venue. People file in, excited and oblivious.
base.first_entry_text:
Well that was weird. A[color=#5086ce] {appearance} [/color]named[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]is completely losing their mind near the entrance. You sigh and look at the crowd. It's busier than you expected.

Do you try to help and chance looking as crazy as {visionary}? Or do you keep your head down and hope for the best?

objects.name: car radio
objects.description: A battered car nearby is blaring their radio for all to hear.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
The crowd is filing in around you. Excited. Oblivious. You're standing at the entrance and you cannot move.

The images are louder here. Closer. You can see exactly where everyone will be standing when it happens.

You have to get them out.

[color=ff4444]You have maybe minutes.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The main entrance. People stream past you, laughing, checking their phones. You know what this place looks like after. You can't unsee it.

public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.name: Grand Theater Foyer
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.description: The ornate, gilded lobby of the theater. The muffled sound of the orchestra bleeds through the heavy velvet doors. There is a faint, rhythmic vibration in the marble floor—the distant, heavy hum of the massive hydraulic systems powering the stage.

examine_details.velvet_doors: Heavy soundproofing keeps the lobby relatively quiet, but you can feel the bass of the sound system vibrating the brass handles.
examine_details.crystal_chandelier: A massive, expensive fixture hanging from the ceiling. It trembles ever so slightly with the vibrations from the stage machinery.

carnival.name: McKinley Fairgrounds — Main Gate
carnival.description: A wrought-iron archway reads McKINLEY COUNTY FAIR in bulb letters, half of them burned out. The smell of funnel cake and livestock hits you immediately. Framing the park against the night sky are the two massive titans of the midway: The Demon's Peak Rollercoaster, and the towering Ferris Wheel.
carnival.first_entry_text:
You're waiting to get your tickets checked when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] suddenly drops to their knees, clutching their head and screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the sky falling into the tracks.'[/color]

Security is rushing over. The crowd is irritated. Behind the gates, the carnival continues its loud, rhythmic, mechanical churning. You can hear the coaster train clicking up the lift hill, and the heavy groaning of the Ferris Wheel turning.

examine_details.fair_archway: McKINLEY COUNTY FAIR — 47th ANNUAL. The bulbs flicker erratically.
examine_details.looming_rides: The Demon's Peak coaster tracks weave dangerously close to the massive central supports of the Ferris Wheel. A terrifying feat of cramped engineering.
examine_details.lost_and_found_box: A cardboard box labeled LOST & FOUND. It contains a single child's shoe.

entertainment_venue.name: Crimson Lounge — Front Door
entertainment_venue.description: A neon sign buzzes overhead: CRIMSON LOUNGE. The bass from inside throbs through the walls. A bouncer checks IDs under a red heat lamp.
entertainment_venue.first_entry_text:
You were handing your ID to the bouncer when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]shoved past you, hysterical, claiming the building was going to burn down.

The bouncer throws them to the pavement. The line halts. You step back to avoid the scuffle.

examine_details.neon_sign: CRIMSON LOUNGE. The 'I' flickers. The red glow makes everyone look feverish.
examine_details.fire_exit_sign: A small green sign above a side door. The door has a chain and padlock on it. That can't be legal.
examine_details.drink_specials_board: 'TONIGHT: $5 FIREBALLS.' An unfortunate name, given what's coming.

public_venue_accident.name: Downtown Plaza — South Entrance
public_venue_accident.description: A bustling open-air plaza surrounded by high-rises. Office workers eat lunch on benches. A street musician plays saxophone. Forty stories above, a window-washing platform crawls along the glass face of the Meridian Tower.
public_venue_accident.first_entry_text:
You were walking into the plaza for lunch when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]grabbed your shoulders, eyes wild, telling you to run.

They pushed you back toward the street just as a street performer stopped playing. They're looking up. Way up.

examine_details.meridian_tower: A gleaming glass skyscraper. Forty stories of corporate ambition. A window-washing platform hangs from cables near the 35th floor.
examine_details.platform_overhead: A metal platform suspended by two cables, slowly traversing the building face. Two workers in harnesses stand on it. One of the cables looks... taut. More than the other.
examine_details.street_musician: A saxophonist playing something melancholy. His case is open. A few bills and a lot of coins.

public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.name: Downtown Plaza — South Entrance
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.description: A bustling open-air plaza surrounded by high-rises. Office workers eat lunch on benches. A street musician plays saxophone. Forty stories above, a window-washing platform crawls along the glass face of the Meridian Tower.
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You were walking into the plaza for lunch when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]grabbed your shoulders, eyes wild, telling you to run.

They pushed you back toward the street just as a street performer stopped playing. They're looking over at the escalators with a concerned look.

examine_details.meridian_tower: A gleaming glass skyscraper. Forty stories of corporate ambition. A window-washing platform hangs from cables near the 35th floor.
examine_details.platform_overhead: A metal platform suspended by two cables, slowly traversing the building face. Two workers in harnesses stand on it. One of the cables looks... taut. More than the other.
examine_details.street_musician: A saxophonist playing something melancholy. His case is open. A few bills and a lot of coins.

base.name: Main Floor
base.description: The heart of the venue. This is where everyone gathers. This is where it happens.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
This is where most of them die.

You know that with the same certainty you know your own name. The crowd is thick here. Happy. Completely unaware.

You need to start moving people [color=ff4444]right now.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The main floor, packed with people who have no idea what you know. Every face here is a decision: do you try to warn them, or do you keep moving?

carnival.name: Midway — Central
carnival.description: The midway stretches in both directions, an assault of noise and flashing lights. To your left, the Ferris wheel turns slowly. To your right, the Demon's Peak coaster roars past. The coaster's track actually threads *between* the giant A-frame support legs of the Ferris wheel.
carnival.first_entry_text:
The midway is a wall of noise — carnival barkers, screaming kids, ride motors, pop music from competing speakers.

[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is hyperventilating, staring up at the two massive rides. 'They're too close together,' they mutter. 'If one goes...'

examine_details.engineering_nightmare: The clearance between the coaster's corkscrew and the Ferris wheel's structural supports is barely ten feet. One structural failure would turn both rides into a blender.
examine_details.strength_game: A tall wooden tower with a bell at the top. 'RING THE BELL, WIN A PRIZE!'
examine_details.prize_wall: Rows of oversized stuffed animals. Their dead plastic eyes stare at nothing.

entertainment_venue.name: Crimson Lounge — Main Floor
entertainment_venue.description: A packed dance floor throbs with bass. Laser lights cut through artificial fog. The bar runs the entire length of the far wall. Emergency exits are marked but the signs are barely visible through the haze.
entertainment_venue.first_entry_text:
The music hits you like a physical force. Bodies everywhere. The fog machine is working overtime — you can barely see the far wall.

Someone grabs your arm. A stranger. Wide eyes. They're trying to tell you something but you can't hear over the music.

examine_details.blocked_fire_exit: A fire exit door. Blocked by a stack of amplifier cases. A fire marshal would have a seizure.
examine_details.fog_machine: A commercial fog machine pumping out thick white clouds. The haze is so dense you can taste it.
examine_details.laser_rig: A rack of laser projectors on the ceiling. One of them is sparking slightly. Nobody seems to notice.

public_venue_accident.name: {city_name} Plaza — Center
public_venue_accident.description: The heart of the plaza. A large fountain sprays water into the air. Office workers sit on the fountain's edge eating sandwiches. The shadow of the Meridian Tower falls across the western half.
public_venue_accident.first_entry_text:
You find a spot near the fountain. The sun is warm. Normal. A completely normal lunch.

A street performer nearby suddenly stops playing. They're looking up. Way up.

'Something's wrong with that platform,' they say to nobody in particular.

examine_details.platform_cables: From here you can see both cables. The left one is vibrating. The platform is tilting — barely, maybe two degrees. The workers on it haven't noticed yet.
examine_details.tower_shadow: The shadow of the Meridian Tower cuts across the plaza like a sundial. You're sitting right in its path.
examine_details.lunch_crowd: Dozens of people eating, talking, scrolling their phones. None of them are looking up.

public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Plaza — Center
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.description: The heart of the plaza. A large fountain sprays water into the air. Office workers sit on the fountain's edge eating sandwiches. The shadow of the Meridian Tower falls across the western half.
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You find a spot near the fountain. The sun is warm. Normal. A completely normal lunch.

A street performer nearby suddenly stops playing. They're looking up. Way up.

'Something's wrong with that platform,' they say to nobody in particular.

examine_details.platform_cables: From here you can see both cables. The left one is vibrating. The platform is tilting — barely, maybe two degrees. The workers on it haven't noticed yet.
examine_details.tower_shadow: The shadow of the Meridian Tower cuts across the plaza like a sundial. You're sitting right in its path.
examine_details.lunch_crowd: Dozens of people eating, talking, scrolling their phones. None of them are looking up.

public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.name: Orchestra Seating
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.description: A packed house. All eyes are drawn forward to the massive proscenium arch. Above the performers, in the shadows of the fly space, tons of steel lighting rigs and speaker arrays hang suspended by taut cables. It's a marvel of engineering, hanging directly over hundreds of heads.

examine_details.proscenium_arch: An intricately carved arch framing the stage. If you listen closely under the music, you can hear a faint, metallic groan echoing from the support columns.
examine_details.lighting_rigs: Hundreds of heavy fixtures mounted on steel trusses. Every time the heavy bass hits, you swear you see them sway against their tethers.

base.name: Side Area
base.description: A quieter area adjacent to the main floor. Fewer people. Different atmosphere.

carnival.name: Animal Barns
carnival.description: The smell hits you first — hay, manure, and hot animal bodies. Prize bulls stand in metal pens. A massive Brahma bull stamps and snorts in the far stall. A frayed rope is the only thing between it and the walkway.

examine_details.prize_bull: A massive Brahma bull. Muscle and fury. Its eyes track you with an intelligence that makes your skin crawl.
examine_details.frayed_rope: The rope holding the pen gate is worn to threads. One good pull — from a bull or a person — and it snaps.
examine_details.barn_door: A large sliding barn door. The track is rusted. It would take effort to close quickly.

entertainment_venue.name: VIP Lounge
entertainment_venue.description: A roped-off section with leather couches and bottle service. Quieter than the main floor, but the bass still pulses through the walls.

examine_details.private_exit: A door marked STAFF ONLY. It's unlocked. It leads to the alley behind the building.
examine_details.VIP_curtain: Heavy velvet curtain separating the VIP area from the main floor. Through the gap, you can see the fog getting thicker.

public_venue_accident.name: Meridian Tower Lobby
public_venue_accident.description: The ground-floor lobby of the Meridian Tower. Marble floors, security desk, elevator bank. A maintenance door is propped open with a fire extinguisher.

examine_details.cable_access_panel: A panel on the wall labeled PLATFORM CABLE CONTROL — AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. It's slightly ajar. Inside, you can see cable tension readouts. One gauge is in the red.
examine_details.maintenance_door: A heavy door propped open. Beyond it, a stairwell. A sign reads: ROOF ACCESS — PLATFORM OPERATIONS.
examine_details.building_directory: Floors 1-15: Corporate offices. Floor 38: Platform staging. Roof: Cable motor housing.

public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.name: Stage Left Access Corridor
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.description: A dimly lit, restricted access hallway. A tangle of thick black power cables and braided hydraulic lines run along the walls up to the fly loft. The air here smells sharply of ozone and hot machine grease.

examine_details.hydraulic_lines: They are vibrating violently under immense pressure. One of the main line joints is leaking a slow, steady drip of highly flammable amber fluid.
examine_details.utility_panel: A small electrical box on the wall. A red indicator labeled 'WINCH OVERLOAD' is flickering faintly. No one seems to be watching it.

base.name: Food and Drink
base.description: A place to eat and drink. The food looks questionable. The drinks are overpriced.

carnival.name: Food Court
carnival.description: A cluster of food stalls selling deep-fried everything. Propane tanks sit beneath the fryers. A generator hums nearby, its exhaust vent pointing at the seating area.

examine_details.propane_rack: Three large propane tanks connected to the fryers by rubber hoses. One hose has a visible crack. Nobody seems concerned.
examine_details.extension_cord_tangle: A rat's nest of extension cords, some taped together. They're plugged into a single power strip that's warm to the touch.
examine_details.grease_puddle: A slick of old fryer grease on the ground near the stall. A clear slip hazard.

entertainment_venue.name: Crimson Lounge — Bar
entertainment_venue.description: The long bar is three deep with people shouting drink orders. Bottles line the wall behind the bartender. The cocktail menu features something called a 'Lit Fuse.' It arrives on fire.

examine_details.overloaded_outlet: Behind the bar, a single wall outlet has six plugs jammed into it via adapters. The plastic is slightly melted.
examine_details.cocktail_menu: 'Lit Fuse — $12. Served FLAMING.' A bartender pours one. The blue flame reflects in his eyes.
examine_details.spilled_drink: Someone knocked over a drink. It's pooling near the DJ booth's power cables.

public_venue_accident.name: Plaza Food Trucks
public_venue_accident.description: A row of food trucks lines the western edge of the plaza, directly beneath the Meridian Tower. The lunch rush is at its peak.

examine_details.shadow_line: The shadow of the Meridian Tower bisects the food truck area. Half the trucks are in shade. You notice the shadow is moving — or rather, the platform's shadow within it is. It's swaying.
examine_details.queue_of_people: A line of twenty people waiting for tacos. All of them standing directly below the window-washing platform.
examine_details.truck_awning: A fabric awning stretched over the seating area. It wouldn't stop a falling wrench, let alone a falling platform.

public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.name: Mezzanine Concessions
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.description: The carpeted bar area just outside the upper seating. It's mostly empty during the performance, save for a few latecomers. The air conditioning vents here are pumping out a subtle, lingering smell of sulfur from the show's opening pyrotechnics.

examine_details.ventilation_grate: Blowing cold air mixed with the distinct, acrid smell of burnt flash powder.
examine_details.bar_counter: Sticky with spilled drinks. The glasses stacked behind the counter are rattling faintly against each other from the structural vibrations.

base.name: Overlook
base.description: A vantage point. From here, you see the moment everything goes wrong.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
From up here you can see the whole floor. You can see exactly where the structural failure begins.

You have a better view of the disaster than anyone else in this building. That is not a comfort.
visionary_player.description: An elevated area overlooking the main floor. From here, the geography of the disaster is laid out in front of you like a map.

carnival+heights.name: Ferris Wheel Base
carnival+heights.description: The base of the Ferris wheel. The motor house is at eye level from here. It smells of hot metal and grease.
carnival+heights.first_entry_text:
You were just getting sat in the gondola when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]had a complete meltdown. The operator had to halt the ride and unlock the carriage to drag them out. You stepped off too, mostly out of secondhand embarrassment.

You'd debated getting back on just to prove you weren't scared, but you walked away instead - the whole ferris wheel moment had passed for you.
Then the wheel starts turning again. The coaster is roaring through its loop.

A vibration travels down the struts as you continue on. The Ferris wheel motor pitch is wrong — too high, too strained.

A bolt ejects from the central hub, pinging off the concrete. You look back as the main axle shears.

The massive wheel drops, buckling its A-frame supports. But it doesn't just fall—it starts to roll.

The colossal ring of steel and screaming passengers crushes the ticket booth, tips forward, one side buckles, and [color=#FF3131]the wheel topples directly into the track of the Demon's Peak coaster.[/color]

The timing is a masterclass in cruelty. The coaster train, moving at 60mph, comes out of a high dip and plows headfirst into the collapsed Ferris wheel structure. The train derails instantly, the cars launching off the mangled track like mortar shells. [color=#ffff00]Gondolas from the wheel and cars from the coaster[/color] collide mid-air, raining a devastating shrapnel of fibreglass, steel, and bodies across the entire midway.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was right. The rides didn't just break; they weaponized each other.

examine_details.ferris_wheel_base: The base casting has split. You can see where the metal fatigued — a grey, granular fracture that's been growing for years.
examine_details.fallen_bolt: M24 hex bolt. Grade 8. The threads are stripped and the shaft shows torsional fatigue shear. This bolt has been failing for a long time.
examine_details.operator_booth: The operator is gone. The emergency stop button has been mashed flat. It didn't work.

carnival+mechanical_failure.name: Fairgrounds Entrance
carnival+mechanical_failure.description: A wrought-iron archway reads McKINLEY COUNTY FAIR in bulb letters, half of them burned out. The smell of funnel cake and livestock hits you immediately. Families stream through turnstiles.
carnival+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
[color=00ff00]You were strapped into the front car.[/color] The lap bar was locked. Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started thrashing and screaming that the ride was going to crash. The operators had to unlock the whole train to get them out. You and everyone else had to get off while security came and did their thing, but they were letting people back on afterwards, if they wanted.

You left, and by the time you had reached the park exit, the first car crests the lift hill. The chain disengages. The car descends, rockets upwards into its first big turn, and.. 

[color=ffff00]..the front car's wheels break free[/color], causing sparks to shower the passengers, while [color=ffb366]all of the safety harnesses are disengaged[/color] from hydraulic failure. The coaster hits the sharp curve at fifty miles per hour, and [color=ff4444]the first section of cars leaves the tracks.[/color]

The massive chain of coaster cars launches through the air and [color=#FF3131]slams broadside into the structural support column of the Ferris Wheel.[/color]

The impact shears the steel support clean in half. The Ferris Wheel, fully loaded with passengers, groans in protest before its central hub buckles under the uneven weight. The towering wheel collapses sideways, its massive rim crushing the Midway, a section of coaster ahead of the remaining cars, and the food stalls in a horrific, slow-motion avalanche of twisting metal and arcing electricity.

The two rides have completely annihilated each other, and half the park with them.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was right. The park was a giant, mechanical mousetrap.

examine_details.coaster_wreckage_view: One car is embedded in the support structure. A second car has derailed further up and soared into the house of mirrors, another two landing nearby in other attractions (like the ferris wheel). The ride is still moving — the chain hasn't stopped.
examine_details.track_gap: You can see the gap from here. Two bolts. That's all. Two bolts and this doesn't happen.

entertainment_venue+fire_related.name: Crimson Lounge — Upper Balcony
entertainment_venue+fire_related.description: A narrow balcony overlooking the main floor. You came up for air. The music is thinner up here and the crowd looks different from above — too dense, too many bodies near the wrong walls.
entertainment_venue+fire_related.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] wouldn't stop screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the smell of burning hair'[/color] even though the air was thick with perfume and expensive gin. They dragged you up to the balcony, away from the pulsing heat of the dance floor, while the crowd below mocked them with drunken laughter.

Directly beneath you, a bartender slides a cocktail across the polished wood. It’s a [color=#FF3131]'Lit Fuse'[/color]—a signature move they've performed a thousand times without incident. 

Not this time. 

The high-proof spill ignites a paper napkin, which licks against the [color=#aaaaaa]decorative foam padding[/color] behind the bar. It doesn't just burn; the chemicals in the foam [color=#FF3131]detonate into a liquid fire[/color]. In three seconds, the ceiling is a [color=#FF3131]roaring lake of orange heat[/color]. In eight seconds, the entire back wall has vanished behind a [color=#aaaaaa]choking, black curtain of toxic plastic smoke[/color].

The bass-heavy music is cut short by a collective, [color=#FF3131]animal shriek[/color] as the crowd surges for the front doors. From the balcony, you have a bird's-eye view of the [color=#aaaaaa]Design[/color]: the main exit is completely choked by a massive stack of fallen speaker equipment.
You safely exit and watch from ground level through busted, barred windows as the bodies begin to [color=#FF3131]pile up against the dead weight of the amps[/color], the exit becoming a [color=#aaaaaa]sealed furnace[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] saw the sparks before the match was even struck. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]trampled and charred[/color] in that crush.

examine_details.floor_view: The main floor is unrecognisable. The fog machine ruptured and its fuel is burning. The fire suppression system discharged once and stopped. The blocked exit has become a killing ground.
examine_details.balcony_railing: The railing is getting hot. The heat is rising fast. You need to move.
examine_details.emergency_strobe: The strobes are firing — red, white, red, white — which makes it impossible to see clearly. Which is probably what's killing people right now.

public_venue_accident.name: Plaza East — Safe Distance
public_venue_accident.description: The eastern edge of the plaza. You crossed the street. Something told you to move. You didn't question it.
public_venue_accident.first_entry_text:
[color=BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] dragged you across the street, away from the plaza, ranting about falling glass and metal.

You hear it before you see it.

A cable under tension makes a specific sound when it's about to fail — a high, singing note, [color=FF3131]like a guitar string wound too tight.[/color]

The window-washing platform drops. Not gradually — one end cuts loose and [color=FF3131]the whole thing pivots in a screaming arc.[/color] Tools fall first; wrenches, squeegees, a metal bucket spinning end over end. The bucket slams into the ground while various tools find new homes [color=FF3131]embedded in the skulls of people looking up at the noise.[/color]

Then the platform hits the food truck row like a blade. Propane tanks normally used to cook food suddenly become lethal as [color=FF3131]multiple explosions[/color] rock the plaza.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] knew. How the hell did they know??

examine_details.impact_zone_view: Where the food trucks were, there is now a compressed mass of steel and aluminum. One of the workers was still on the platform. The other got clear.
examine_details.scattered_debris: A hard hat. A squeegee with the handle snapped. A full cup of coffee, still upright on the sidewalk, completely untouched.

public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.name: Mall Upper Level — Balcony Rail
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.description: The upper concourse balcony. You leaned on the rail, looking down at the escalator bank. Something about the sound was wrong — a grinding underneath the music.
public_venue_accident+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You stepped back from the escalator when[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started shouting at people to stay off it.

The grinding sound was coming from inside the machine — the shaft key failing — and the motor revs as the escalator shoots forward.

The line of shoppers, traffic-jammed behind a person with some shiny new luggage, are thrown into a pile at the bottom, where their combined weight breaks the floor panels loose, one by one.

The escalator stops for a moment - mangled metal unable to keep in fluid motion - until the motor malfunctions and repeatedly bucks in the *opposite* direction, throwing people forward before grinding backwards through clothing, shopping bags, and eventually skin and bone, over and over. The people on the ascending escalator have less than a second to process, and then the floor panels at the escalator bottom give, dropping patrons into the internal machinery.

You watch from the balcony rail. You can't look away as the escalator turns into a meat grinder. You can't do anything.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] really knew.
How the FUCK

examine_details.escalator_view: The escalator has stopped. Mall security has formed a cordon. You can hear one siren, then three, then too many to count.
examine_details.balcony_railing: Your knuckles are white where you're gripping it. You've been standing here for two minutes and you haven't moved.

public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.name: Theater Mezzanine — Rear Exit
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.description: The rear mezzanine exit. You stood up to leave during the interval. You're not sure why. The finale was supposed to be spectacular.
public_venue_accident+structural_collaps.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was hysterical, clawing their way over people in the aisle to get to the exit. You chased after them just to get them to quiet down.

You hear the rigging before you see it fail.

A groan from above — not the show, not the music, something structural — and then the proscenium arch shifts. One of the hydraulic motors has seized. The entire fly system is under load it wasn't built to carry.

The arch comes down in sections. The first section takes the lighting rig. The second takes the performers. The third section collapses into the orchestra pit.

The pyrotechnics fire on their programmed cue. Into the dust cloud.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was right.

examine_details.stage_debris_view: The stage is gone — buried under tons of lighting rigs, speaker arrays, and rigging hardware. The hydraulic fluid is burning. The curtains are burning. The playbills are burning.
examine_details.emergency_exit_sign: The exit sign is still lit. Green. A small mercy, pointing at the door you're already walking through.

visionary_player+carnival+heights.first_entry_text:
You screamed at the gondola operator to stop the ride.

You'd seen it. The exact angle of the car when the locking pin shears. The exact moment the gondola inverts. You made enough noise that the operator actually halted the line — and when the carriage unlocked you grabbed the people nearest to you and pulled them out.

They're angry. You're up on the overlook now, away from the queue, watching the ride restart below, watching the line reform.

The locking pin shears. [color=#FF3131]You knew exactly when it would.[/color]

visionary_player+carnival+mechanical_fai.first_entry_text:
You were already screaming before you fully understood why.

The front car. The lap bar that wasn't going to hold. The vibration in the track you could feel through your shoes that nobody else seemed to notice.

You thrashed until the operators stopped the whole train. Until everyone had to get off. Security escorted you out of the queue.

You're watching from the overlook when the [color=#FF3131]crash happens exactly as you saw it.[/color] From up here it looks small. It isn't small.

visionary_player+entertainment_venue+fir.first_entry_text:
You could smell it before anyone else could.

Not smoke — the [color=#FF3131]smell of burning hair and hot copper[/color], the specific scent of electrical insulation failing inside the walls. You pulled people away from the dance floor. You dragged them toward the balcony exits, screaming about a fire that hadn't started yet.

They mocked you. You kept moving.

From up here, when the [color=#FF3131]first flame appears in the ceiling rig[/color], you watch the crowd below realize you were right.

visionary_player+public_venue_accident.first_entry_text:
You were already moving before the images fully formed.

Falling glass. The specific weight of metal coming down. You grabbed people by the arms and walked them across the street, talking fast, not making sense, not caring.

A cable under tension makes a specific sound when it's about to fail — [color=#FF3131]a high, singing note, like a guitar string wound too tight.[/color]

You hear it. You've heard it before, in the images. You're already watching from the overlook when the cable goes.

visionary_player+public_venue_accident+m.first_entry_text:
You stood in front of the escalator.

Physically. In the way. Refusing to move. You could feel the shaft key failing through the handrail — a specific, rhythmic [color=#FF3131]wrongness in the vibration[/color] — and you shouted at everyone to stay off until security arrived to deal with you instead.

They pulled you aside. The escalator kept running.

You hear the motor rev from the overlook. You see the [color=#FF3131]line of shoppers cascade[/color] like a wave. You already knew how many.

visionary_player+public_venue_accident+s.first_entry_text:
You clawed your way over people in the aisle.

Not because you were panicking — because you had already seen exactly what the rigging looked like when it failed, and you needed everyone near the stage to not be there when it happened.

They thought you were hysterical. You kept moving.

You hear the groan from up here — [color=#FF3131]something structural, not the show[/color] — and then the rigging comes down exactly where you'd seen it come down in the images you've been carrying since last night.

roadside._description: Linear highway/rural flow: vehicle/roadside → approach → work zone/site → shoulder overlook. Covers highways, pipelines, factories, grain silos.

base.name: Vehicle Stop
base.description: You've pulled over on the shoulder. Gravel crunches under your tires. The engine ticks as it cools.
base.first_entry_text: You pull off the road.[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]made everybody stop, screaming from the backseat. What is going on?

objects.name: car radio
objects.description: A battered car nearby is blaring their radio for all to hear.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
The roadside stop. Trucks and cars and people who are about to be in exactly the wrong place.

You know the vehicle. You know the trajectory. You don't know how to explain any of this to someone who hasn't seen what you've seen.

[color=ff4444]Start with the people closest to the road.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The roadside area. Traffic moves in both directions. You know which vehicle is going to lose control and when.

rural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Dirt Shoulder — Co-op Entrance
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: You've pulled your car onto the dirt shoulder outside the McKinley Grain Co-op. The massive concrete silos tower over the fields. The air is thick with agricultural dust.
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.first_entry_text: [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] made you slam on the brakes, screaming about the silos detonating. You can see thick plumes of grain dust venting from the roof.

examine_details.looming_silos: Massive concrete cylinders storing millions of tons of grain. They are making a low, pressurized thrumming sound.
examine_details.field_view: Acres of dry, dead cornstalks waiting to be harvested. A massive fire hazard.

transportation_road+impact_blunt.name: Route 42 — Emergency Shoulder
transportation_road+impact_blunt.description: Your car sits on the narrow shoulder of Route 42. Three lanes of traffic crawl past, bumper to bumper. The air smells like exhaust and hot asphalt. A semi-truck's air brakes hiss somewhere ahead.
transportation_road+impact_blunt.first_entry_text:
Traffic is dead stopped thanks to[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]throwing the car into park and jumping out onto the shoulder.

You get out to stretch your legs and yell at them. The pavement radiates heat.

examine_details.traffic_ahead: A solid wall of vehicles stretching to the horizon. Brake lights blink in sequence like a slow heartbeat.
examine_details.highway_sign: ROUTE 42 NORTH — McKINLEY 12 MI. Someone spray-painted 'GOOD LUCK' under it.
examine_details.heat_shimmer: The asphalt ripples with heat. It's at least 95 degrees out here.

industrial_accident+machinery.name: Factory Parking Lot
industrial_accident+machinery.description: The asphalt lot outside the main factory. The rumble of heavy machinery inside vibrates through the soles of your shoes.
industrial_accident+machinery.first_entry_text: You were walking toward the main entrance when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] bolted out the double doors, hyperventilating and screaming about the roof caving in.

examine_details.factory_exterior: A massive block of corrugated steel and aging brick. It looks like it was built in the 1940s and barely maintained since.
examine_details.loading_bays: Several semi-trucks are backed up to the loading docks. Forklifts dart in and out.

industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.name: Access Road — Plant Perimeter
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.description: A two-lane access road runs along the chain-link perimeter fence of the McKinley industrial complex. Warning signs line the fence every twenty feet. A security booth sits unmanned at the gate.
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.first_entry_text:
You drove out here because[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]threatened to jump out of the moving car if you went any closer to the plant.

The industrial complex looms behind the fence — pipes, tanks, cooling towers.

The security booth is empty. The gate is open.

examine_details.warning_signs: DANGER — AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY. COMBUSTIBLE MATERIALS. NO SMOKING WITHIN 500 FT.
examine_details.plant_skyline: A maze of pipes, valves, and storage tanks. Steam rises from a cooling tower. Everything looks operational. Everything looks fine.
examine_details.open_gate: The gate is wide open. The chain and padlock lie on the ground, cut clean through.

industrial_accident+geological_event.name: Access Road — Shoulder
industrial_accident+geological_event.description: The gravel shoulder of a desolate industrial access road. Your car is parked haphazardly half-off the asphalt. The air is thick with the suffocating smell of sulphur.
industrial_accident+geological_event.first_entry_text:
You were making good time down the access road when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] woke up screaming, lunging across the console to grab the steering wheel. The tires locked up, spraying gravel as you skidded to a violent halt.

Now they are hyperventilating, staring terrified out the windshield at the empty stretch of road ahead.

[color=#FF3131]"We have to get out! NOW!"[/color] they scream, practically tearing at their seatbelt.

The only sound outside is the wind through the dry grass. But as you crack your window, a smell creeps into the cabin. Something like rotten eggs and raw, churned earth.

examine_details.idling car: Your car sits at a crooked angle in the dirt. The engine is still running, pulling the noxious outside air straight into the AC vents.
examine_details.drainage culvert: A large concrete pipe running under the road just ahead of you. The air above the grate is physically rippling, like a thick heat haze. The smell of gas here is so strong it is making your eyes water.
examine_details.approaching truck: In the rearview mirror, a heavy utility truck is barreling down the road toward your position. You can faintly see the driver resting his arm casually out the open window.

base.name: Approach Road
base.description: A stretch of road leading toward the site. You can see activity ahead.

rural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Co-op Access Road
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: A gravel access road leading directly to the base of the silos. A line of heavy transport trucks waits to unload their harvests.

examine_details.idling_trucks: Diesel exhaust mixes with the grain dust. A highly combustible combination.
examine_details.dust_clouds: The grain dust is so thick here you can taste it. It coats everything in a fine, yellow-white powder.

transportation_road+impact_blunt.name: Route 42 — Between Vehicles
transportation_road+impact_blunt.description: You weave between idling cars and trucks. Drivers sit with windows down, radios playing different stations. A tanker truck's engine rumbles. The shoulder drops off into a drainage ditch.

examine_details.tanker_truck: A massive fuel tanker. FLAMMABLE stenciled on the side in faded red letters. The tank is sweating in the heat — condensation, or a slow leak?
examine_details.drainage_ditch: A concrete drainage channel running parallel to the highway. Bone dry. If you needed to get off the road fast, this would be the way.
examine_details.frustrated_drivers: People leaning on horns, scrolling phones, shouting into the air. One guy is standing on his car roof trying to see what's ahead.

industrial_accident+machinery.name: Factory Walkway
industrial_accident+machinery.description: A concrete walkway leading to the main factory floor. The rhythmic, thunderous thud of the stamping presses echoes loudly here.

examine_details.warning_signs: HARD HAT AND SAFETY GLASSES REQUIRED BEYOND THIS POINT. The sign is covered in a thick layer of industrial dust.
examine_details.forklift_crossing: Yellow lines painted on the concrete. The ground shakes with every cycle of the machines inside.

industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.name: Plant Road — Loading Area
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.description: A wide loading area inside the perimeter. Forklifts sit idle. Pallets of chemical drums are stacked against a warehouse wall. A pressure gauge on a nearby pipe reads well into the red zone.

examine_details.chemical_drums: Blue plastic drums with hazmat diamonds. The labels read SODIUM HYDROXIDE — CORROSIVE. One drum has a dark stain spreading from its base.
examine_details.pressure_gauge: The needle is pinned against the right side of the dial. The red zone starts at 150 PSI. This reads 210. The pipe is vibrating.
examine_details.forklift: Keys in the ignition. The seat is still warm.

base.name: Rest Area
base.description: A small pulloff area with basic amenities. A place to regroup.

rural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Weigh Station
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: A small, windowed weigh station for the grain trucks. The operator is inside, arguing on a telephone.
