examine_details.warning_sign: DANGER: COMBUSTIBLE DUST. NO SMOKING. NO OPEN FLAMES. Someone has stubbed out a cigarette directly on the sign.

transportation_road+impact_blunt.name: Route 42 — Emergency Callbox
transportation_road+impact_blunt.description: A small emergency pulloff with a callbox and a faded highway map under plexiglass. Someone has left a half-eaten sandwich on the guardrail.

examine_details.emergency_phone: A blue emergency phone. You pick it up — dead. The cable has been cut. Cleanly.
examine_details.accident_report_board: A faded DOT sign: THIS STRETCH OF ROUTE 42 HAS HAD 14 FATALITIES IN THE LAST 3 YEARS. DRIVE SAFE. Someone crossed out 14 and wrote 15.

industrial_accident+machinery.name: Worker Break Area
industrial_accident+machinery.description: A chain-link enclosure with a few picnic tables for smokers. An overflowing ashtray sits in the center. The roar of the machines inside is muffled but ever-present.

examine_details.safety_bulletin: A corkboard with OSHA regulations. Someone has tacked a handwritten note over it complaining about the roof leaking near the heavy presses.

industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.name: Plant Break Room (Exterior)
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.description: A concrete slab with a picnic table and a coffee machine that's seen better decades. Safety posters peel from the wall. A whiteboard reads: DAYS WITHOUT INCIDENT: 0.

examine_details.safety_poster: A cartoon worker gives a thumbs up. 'SAFETY IS NO ACCIDENT!' The irony is suffocating.
examine_details.incident_board: DAYS WITHOUT INCIDENT: The '0' is written in fresh marker. The previous number has been erased so many times the whiteboard is grey.
examine_details.hard_hat_rack: Three hard hats. One has a crack running through it. Someone put a smiley face sticker over the crack.

base.name: Work Zone
base.description: The center of activity. This is where it's about to happen.

transportation_road+impact_blunt.name: Route 42 — Merge Point
transportation_road+impact_blunt.description: Three lanes squeeze into one. Orange cones funnel traffic into a bottleneck. A construction crew works behind a concrete barrier. A crane arm swings overhead. Everything is too close together.

examine_details.merge_bottleneck: Cars, trucks, and semis jammed nose-to-tail trying to merge. Tempers are visible through windshields. A motorcycle weaves between lanes.
examine_details.crane_overhead: A construction crane swings a concrete slab over the traffic. The cable creaks. Workers below don't even look up anymore.
examine_details.concrete_barrier: Temporary jersey barriers separating traffic from the work zone. They look about as sturdy as cardboard.

industrial_accident+machinery.name: Main Factory Floor
industrial_accident+machinery.description: A cavernous hall of catwalks, conveyor belts, and massive industrial presses. The noise is deafening. Looking up, the heavy steel roof trusses appear severely bowed under the weight of the overhead cranes.

examine_details.roof_trusses: Thick steel beams spanning the ceiling. One of the central trusses has a visible, jagged crack running through its mounting plate.
examine_details.stamping_presses: Massive hydraulic machines stamping sheet metal. The floor physically drops a fraction of an inch every time they hit.

industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.name: Plant Floor — Reactor Building
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.description: The main reactor building. Pipes run in every direction. The hum of machinery is so deep you feel it in your teeth. A control panel blinks amber warnings that nobody is watching.

examine_details.reactor_core_view: Through a reinforced window, the core of the operation — pressure vessels, heat exchangers, a web of pipes. One pipe is glowing dull red. That can't be normal.
examine_details.control_panel: Dozens of gauges and switches. Three amber warnings flash: PRESSURE ANOMALY, TEMP EXCEEDANCE, COOLANT LOW. A fourth light is solid red: MANUAL OVERRIDE ENGAGED.
examine_details.steam_vent: A vent blasting steam sideways. The steam smells wrong — acrid, chemical. Not just water.

base.name: Overlook Point
base.description: A vantage point. From here, you see the moment it all goes wrong.

transportation_road+construction_failure.name: Route 42 — Overpass
transportation_road+construction_failure.description: You climbed the embankment to the overpass. From here, you can see the entire stretch of Route 42. The merge point. The crane. The tanker.
transportation_road+construction_failure.first_entry_text:
You hauled[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]up the embankment to get them away from the traffic, assuming they were having a panic attack.

Below you, [color=9D00FF]the crane cable snaps.[/color] The concrete slab it was holding drops across two lanes.

The tanker brakes —[color=#b30505] too hard, too fast.[/color] It jackknifes across the median.

The cars behind it don't have time. The pile-up starts slow and ends fast, a chain reaction that moves backward down the highway at sixty miles per hour.

The tanker ignites on the third or seventh impact.

Route 42 becomes a canyon of fire.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] is a fucking witch or something.

examine_details.highway_panorama: The highway is unrecognisable. Black smoke rises in a column visible for miles. Emergency vehicles are staging on the overpass beside you — they can't get to the scene any other way.
examine_details.fireball_below: The tanker fuel spread across four lanes before it ignited. It's still burning. It'll burn for hours.

transportation_road+cargo_spill.name: Route 42 — Overpass
transportation_road+cargo_spill.description: You climbed the embankment to the overpass. From here, you can see the entire stretch of Route 42. A flatbed semi loaded with massive timber logs is cresting the hill.
transportation_road+cargo_spill.first_entry_text:
You hauled [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] up the steep embankment, your boots sliding in the loose gravel as you tried to move them away from the roar of the morning commute. You assumed it was a panic attack—until the [color=#aaaaaa]high-tension steel chains[/color] on a passing logging truck snapped with a [color=#FF3131]bone-deep crack[/color] like a gunshot.

Time dilates as the [color=#aaaaaa]massive timber logs[/color] spill across three lanes at sixty miles per hour. They aren't just obstacles; they are [color=#FF3131]two-ton projectiles[/color]. You watch from above as they skip off the asphalt and [color=#FF3131]obliterate[/color] the cabins of the cars below, peeling back the roofs like [color=#aaaaaa]shredded tin foil[/color].

A chain reaction of [color=#aaaaaa]tortured metal and shrieking tires[/color] piles up behind the spill. Fuel tanks rupture, spraying gasoline into an air thick with [color=#aaaaaa]pulverized glass[/color]. A single spark from the grinding wreckage transforms the highway into a [color=#FF3131]roaring canyon of fire[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the metal fatigue before the first link gave way. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]liquidized[/color] in that initial crush.

examine_details.highway_panorama: The highway is a graveyard of crushed steel and splintered wood. The fires are spreading fast.
examine_details.crushed_vehicles: You can barely recognize the makes and models of the cars under the timber. Nobody inside could have survived that.

transportation_road+weather_event.name: Route 42 — Overpass
transportation_road+weather_event.description: You climbed the embankment to the overpass. From here, you can usually see the entire stretch of Route 42. But today, a thick, unnatural bank of fog is rolling across the valley.
transportation_road+weather_event.first_entry_text:
You hauled [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] up the steep embankment to get them away from the traffic, assuming they were just having a violent panic attack.

Below you, a [color=#A9A9A9]dense, unnatural fog bank[/color] violently swallows the highway. Visibility drops to absolute zero in a matter of seconds.

You don't see the first crash; you just hear it—a [color=#FF3131]sickening, high-velocity crunch[/color] of tearing metal and shattering glass. Then another. Then three at once, echoing blindly through the mist.

Dozens of cars are plowing into an invisible wall of twisted steel at full highway speed. The pileup grows exponentially, a horrifying chorus of blaring horns and screaming tires. Then, a single spark finds a ruptured fuel tank, and the gray fog instantly erupts into a [color=#FF4500]hellish, roaring fireball of burning orange[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was actually right.

examine_details.glowing_fog: The thick fog is illuminated from within by raging fires. You can see the shadows of twisted vehicles within the smoke.
examine_details.highway_sounds: The sounds of tires screeching, metal crunching, and people screaming echo up from the whiteout below.

industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.name: Perimeter Hill — Safe Distance
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.description: A grassy hill outside the plant's perimeter fence. The chain-link is still rattling from the shockwave.
industrial_accident+chemical_hazard.first_entry_text:
You walked out through the open gate and up the hill because[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]physically dragged you.

You were still walking when the pressure relief vents on Reactor Building 3 started screaming.

The walls flex outward. Like a breath. Once.

Then the building peels open from the inside. Not an explosion — a rupture. A contained system releasing everything at once. The roof goes first, spinning into the sky like a dropped coin. The walls follow in segments.

A column of fire reaches the altitude of a small plane.

Secondary explosions chain through the pipe network — each one a new fireball, each one smaller, none of them small.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was right.

examine_details.plant_ruins: Where the reactor building stood, there is now a crater approximately forty feet deep. The pipe network is still exploding in sequence — you can see the fireballs tracking along the underground route.
examine_details.shockwave_debris: The grass between you and the fence is covered in papers. Safety manuals. Inspection logs. A hard hat, cracked clean in half, landed fifteen feet up the hill.

industrial_accident+geological_event.name: Rural Road — Elevated Shoulder
industrial_accident+geological_event.description: The raised shoulder on the east side of the road, up from the drainage culvert. You can smell the gas from here. You could smell it half a mile back.
industrial_accident+geological_event.first_entry_text:
You stopped the car because[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]grabbed the wheel. The smell hit you immediately — sulphur and something underneath it, something that made your teeth ache.

You got out. You walked up the embankment to get away from the fumes.

A truck came down the road. The driver was smoking. The window was open.

The ignition point is three hundred meters behind you. The fireball is the diameter of a city block. The shockwave hits you like a flat hand across the chest and knocks you back two steps.

The pipeline is still burning. It'll burn until someone figures out how to shut the flow off. That'll take hours.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was actually right.

examine_details.pipeline_fire: The trench above the pipeline is burning in both directions along the right-of-way. The nearest structure — a farmhouse — has its windows gone.
examine_details.crater_edge: The initial ignition point has cratered the road by about four feet. The asphalt has melted and re-solidified in strange, rippled shapes.

rural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Grain Co-op Access Road — Field Gate
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: The field gate on the access road. You pulled over here. The silos were making a sound — a deep, pressurised thrumming that you felt more than heard.
rural_disaster+structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] demanded you stop at the gate, refusing to go any closer. The silos were full — you could see it from the road, the grain dust venting from the roof vents in lazy plumes.

Grain dust burns. You knew this, somehow, in the back of your mind.

The static discharge hit somewhere in the conveyor system. The dust cloud inside Silo 3 ignited all at once — not a fire, a deflagration. The silo didn't burn. It detonated.

The shockwave arrived before the noise. It moved the car. Then the noise arrived — a CRACK so loud it wasn't loud anymore, just pressure and white.

Burning grain fell on the fields for two minutes.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was right.

examine_details.silo_crater: Where Silo 3 stood, there is a concrete ring and a crater. The adjacent silos have been deformed by the shockwave — they're still standing but their walls have buckled.
examine_details.burning_fields: The fields downwind of the blast are burning. Grain. Dry stubble. The fence line. A barn a quarter mile away has lost its roof.

industrial_accident+machinery.name: Factory Perimeter — Loading Bay Door
industrial_accident+machinery.description: The loading bay exit at the rear of the building. You came through here. The door is still open behind you.
industrial_accident+machinery.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] grabbed you by the arm and hauled you outside. You told yourself they just needed air.

The floor had been making a sound — a low, periodic flexing, like a drumhead under tension. {visionary} noticed it. Nobody else seemed to.

The roof trusses fail from the centre outward. [color=BC13FE]They don't fall all at once — they cascade, each one releasing load onto the next, the collapse moving across the factory floor like a wave.[/color] The machinery goes with them.

The loading bay doors blow outward from the air pressure of the collapse. You're already outside. [color=FF3131]The door misses you by a meter.[/color]

The dust cloud rolls out across the car park and swallows everything.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was actually right.

examine_details.factory_ruins: The factory is a flat, dense mass of debris. One wall is still standing — the east wall, brick, built in 1937 — but it's leaning and it won't last.
examine_details.dust_front: The dust cloud from the collapse is still moving. It's coating everything in the car park — cars, the gatehouse, you — in a uniform grey.

visionary_player+transportation_road+con.first_entry_text:
You hauled people up the embankment by their arms.

You knew the crane cable was going to snap. You knew the concrete slab would drop across two lanes. You knew the tanker would brake too hard and the trailer would jackknife.

You couldn't explain any of this. You just kept moving people away from the traffic, up the slope, ignoring the shouting.

Below you, [color=9D00FF]the crane cable snaps.[/color]

The concrete slab drops. [color=#FF3131]Exactly where you said it would.[/color]

visionary_player+transportation_road+car.first_entry_text:
You were screaming at people to move before the truck was even visible.

The image had been specific: [color=#aaaaaa]high-tension steel chains[/color] on a logging truck, the exact vibration pattern of a compromised load, the way the logs would spread across the lanes.

You got people up the embankment. Your boots kept sliding in the loose gravel. Nobody helped you.

The chains snap. The logs spread. [color=#FF3131]Every detail exactly right.[/color]

visionary_player+transportation_road+wea.first_entry_text:
You got everyone up the embankment before the fog appeared.

That's the part they won't be able to explain — how you knew to clear the road before the [color=#A9A9A9]dense, unnatural fog bank[/color] swallowed the highway and dropped visibility to absolute zero.

You knew. You've known things like this before, in smaller ways. This time the knowledge was [color=#FF3131]loud.[/color]

From up here, you hear the collision before you hear the horns.

visionary_player+industrial_accident+che.first_entry_text:
You could smell it before the alarms started.

The [color=#FF3131]specific chemical signature of a pressure system failing from the inside[/color] — a sweetness underneath the industrial smell, wrong in a way you felt in your teeth.

You walked out through the open gate and got people up the hill. You told them it was a drill. You told them anything.

The pressure relief vents on Reactor Building 3 start screaming.

The walls flex outward. [color=#FF3131]Once. Like a breath.[/color]

Then the building opens.

visionary_player+industrial_accident+geo.first_entry_text:
You grabbed the wheel.

You don't know exactly why, except that the smell hit you — [color=#FF3131]sulphur and something underneath it, something that made your teeth ache[/color] — and you knew the road ahead was wrong.

You stopped the car. You got people out. You walked them up the embankment while they argued with you.

A truck comes down the road. The driver waves. Then the ground [color=#FF3131]does what you already knew it was going to do.[/color]

visionary_player+rural_disaster+structur.first_entry_text:
You wouldn't go past the gate.

The silos were full — grain dust venting from the roof in lazy plumes — and you knew [color=#FF3131]exactly what static discharge does to suspended grain dust.[/color] You'd seen it. The exact moment the pressure wave hits the walls from inside.

You stopped everyone at the gate. They thought you were being difficult.

The static discharge happens exactly when you said it would. From the road, the silo walls [color=#FF3131]peel outward[/color] like flower petals.

visionary_player+industrial_accident+mac.first_entry_text:
You could feel it through the floor.

A low, periodic flexing — [color=#FF3131]like a drumhead under too much tension[/color] — that you felt in your shoes before you consciously heard it. The kind of thing you only notice when you already know what it means.

You hauled people outside. You told them you needed air. Whatever it took to get them through the door.

The roof trusses fail from the centre, exactly as you saw them fail, in [color=#FF3131]the images you've been carrying since this morning.[/color]

neighborhood._description: Grid layout: street → building entrance → interior → upper floor/rooftop. Covers bridge collapses, apartment collapses, urban structural failures.

base.name: Street Level
base.description: A city street. Traffic passes. People walk. Everything is normal.
base.first_entry_text: You are standing on the street. Everything seems fine.

objects.name: overhead speakers
objects.description: Speakers overhead crackle with muzak and announcements.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
A quiet residential street that will not be quiet for much longer.

You've been standing at the corner for ninety seconds trying to decide where to start. That's ninety seconds you didn't have.

[color=ff4444]Go. Now.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The neighborhood entrance. Ordinary houses. Ordinary people. You know what happens to ordinary neighborhoods when something catastrophic occurs nearby.

geological_event+natural_disaster.name: Hilltop Park — Entrance
geological_event+natural_disaster.description: The paved entrance to the sprawling city park. Strangely, every dog in the vicinity is barking frantically, pulling at their leashes.
geological_event+natural_disaster.first_entry_text:
You arrived at the park to clear your head. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is already here, pacing wildly and claiming the ground is 'screaming'.

A massive flock of birds suddenly erupts from the trees in unison, fleeing the area.

examine_details.fleeing_birds: Hundreds of them, flying erratically. Animals always know before we do.
examine_details.panicked_dogs: A dog walker is struggling to hold onto three golden retrievers that are desperately trying to drag him back toward the street.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: 4th and Main — Intersection
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: A busy downtown intersection. The rain is coming down in blinding sheets. The storm drains are struggling to keep up, and a thick layer of water is already rushing over the curb.
urban_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
You were waiting for the walk signal. The rain was heavy, but it was just a storm. 

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started dragging you by the jacket toward the adjacent parking garage, screaming about the river breaching the retaining wall.

examine_details.rising_water: It's not just rain. The water is rising inches per minute, swirling with brown silt and garbage.
examine_details.gurgling_drain: The storm drain isn't taking water in—it's violently bubbling outward. The city's subterranean pressure is reversing.
examine_details.stranded_cars: Cars are stalled in the intersection, their tires half-submerged. Drivers are honking uselessly.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Memorial Bridge — East Approach
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: The east approach to the {city_name} Memorial Bridge. Two lanes of traffic flow onto the bridge. Pedestrians walk the sidewalk. The bridge stretches across the river, its cables gleaming in the sun.
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You were crossing the bridge on foot. It was a beautiful day — clear sky, light breeze off the river.

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]grabbed you, babbling incoherently, and pulled you back toward the bank.

examine_details.bridge_span: The {city_name} Memorial Bridge. Steel cables, concrete deck, four lanes of traffic. Built in 1974. The plaque says it was 'built to last.' The maintenance schedule might disagree.
examine_details.river_below: Sixty feet down. The {city_name} River is swollen from recent rain. The current looks strong.
examine_details.traffic_flow: Cars and trucks stream onto the bridge. A school bus. A moving van. An ambulance with no sirens. Just another commute.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: Business District — Main Intersection
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: A bustling intersection in the business district. Food carts line the pavement. The asphalt underfoot feels strangely warm, and there is a faint, subterranean rumble.
urban_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
You were walking across the street on your lunch break when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] tugged at your shirt, crying about the ground collapsing.

You were in shock at this complete stranger, but then the pavement actually groaned beneath your boots. A hairline crack shot across the intersection.

examine_details.sagging_asphalt: The center of the intersection is noticeably bowing inward. It looks like a shallow bowl.
examine_details.confused_pedestrians: People are stopping to look at the ground, pointing at the cracks. Nobody is running yet.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: Elm Street — Residential Block
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A quiet residential street lined with old brick apartment buildings. Cars are parallel parked tightly against the curb.
urban_disaster+construction_failure.first_entry_text:
You were walking home. It was a normal day.

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]practically tackled you into the street, screaming at everyone to get away from the buildings.

examine_details.brick_apartments: Six-story brick buildings built in the 1960s. They look solid, but the mortar between the bricks is turning to dust.
examine_details.cracked_pavement: The sidewalk near the foundation is buckling upward slightly.

base.name: Building Entrance
base.description: The entrance to a building. Doors, lobby, the threshold between outside and in.

geological_event+natural_disaster.name: Hilltop Park — Great Lawn
geological_event+natural_disaster.description: A wide expanse of grass. The air is dead still, but you can feel a distinct, high-frequency vibration buzzing through the soil.

examine_details.vibrating_ground: It tickles the soles of your feet. It's not a localized rumble; it feels like the entire tectonic plate is humming.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: Parking Structure — Ground Level
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: The open ground floor of a massive concrete parking garage. The toll barriers are stuck in the down position. Water is rapidly pooling around the tires of the parked cars.
urban_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] hauled you under the concrete overhang just as a surge of brown water swept over the pavement outside. 

'Higher!' they screamed, pointing toward the stairwell. 'We have to get off the ground!'

examine_details.stairwell_door: A heavy metal fire door leading to the stairs. The water is already seeping under the gap.
examine_details.submerged_tires: The cars parked on the ground level are already sitting in a foot of muddy water. A car alarm blares as its electrical system shorts out.
examine_details.toll_booth: Empty. The attendant wisely abandoned their post.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: Bridge Midspan — Pedestrian Walkway
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: The middle of the bridge. The walkway narrows here. You can feel the deck flexing under the traffic. A jogger passes. A mother pushes a stroller. Below, the river churns.

examine_details.deck_surface: The concrete deck has a crack running diagonally across the walkway. You step over it. It's wider than your finger.
examine_details.cable_anchor: A massive steel anchor point where a suspension cable meets the deck. The bolts are rusted. One is missing entirely.
examine_details.inspection_tag: A small metal tag wired to the cable: LAST INSPECTED — 2019. It's been seven years.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: Parking Structure — Ground Floor
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: The ground floor of a massive concrete parking garage. The concrete pillars look solid, but a fine dust is falling from the ceiling.
urban_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] dragged you off the street and into the parking garage. 'Not safe on the street!' they screamed, pointing wildly at the cracks forming on the pavement.

They run toward the stairwell. 'Get off the ground!'

examine_details.concrete_pillars: Thick, load-bearing pillars. You can feel a vibration humming through the concrete.
examine_details.stairwell_door: A heavy metal fire door leading to the stairs. It says 'ROOF ACCESS' on it.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: Apartment Lobby
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A dated lobby with fading floral wallpaper and a row of brass mailboxes. A small elevator sits with its doors open.

examine_details.lobby_ceiling: A long, jagged crack runs from the front door all the way to the elevator. Plaster dust occasionally drifts down from it.
examine_details.mailboxes: Stuffed with junk mail. The brass is tarnished.
examine_details.elevator: An old cage elevator. It groans audibly, even when empty.

base.name: Side Street
base.description: A quieter side street. Less traffic. A few parked cars.

geological_event+natural_disaster.name: Park Gazebo
geological_event+natural_disaster.description: A sturdy wooden gazebo. The timber frame is groaning rhythmically, as if the foundation it sits on is subtly twisting.

examine_details.creaking_timbers: The wood is flexing. The gazebo isn't moving, but the earth beneath its footings is shifting in opposite directions.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: Submerged Alleyway
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: A narrow alley between the parking garage and an office building. The water here is funneling through like a high-speed river channel.

examine_details.floating_dumpster: A heavy steel dumpster has been lifted entirely off the ground by the floodwaters and is slamming repeatedly into a brick wall.
examine_details.rushing_current: The water here is moving terrifyingly fast, choked with debris. Stepping into it would sweep you away instantly.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: River Bank — Under the Bridge
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: A muddy path along the river bank, directly beneath the bridge. The underside of the deck is visible — steel beams, rust stains, pigeon nests. Water drips from somewhere above.

examine_details.bridge_underside: From below, the bridge looks different. The steel beams are streaked with rust. Chunks of concrete have spalled off, exposing rebar. This bridge is sick.
examine_details.support_column: A massive concrete support column. A crack runs vertically from the waterline to the deck. It's been patched with what looks like... duct tape and expanding foam.
examine_details.rust_stains: Orange-brown streaks run down the concrete like tears. The steel is weeping.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: Flooded Alleyway
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: A narrow alleyway behind the business district. A subterranean water main has clearly ruptured—thick, muddy water is bubbling violently up through the storm drains, rapidly washing away the soil beneath the asphalt.

examine_details.bubbling_drains: The water is thick with red clay. The city's foundation is literally liquefying and washing away into the sewers.
examine_details.sinking_dumpster: A heavy metal dumpster is slowly tilting backward as the pavement beneath its wheels sags into the earth.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: Alleyway
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A narrow alley between two apartment buildings. Fire escapes zigzag down the brick walls.

examine_details.brick_wall: The exterior load-bearing wall. It's noticeably bowing outward near the bottom.
examine_details.fire_escape: Rusted metal stairs clinging to the side of the building. They look like they'd rip out of the masonry if more than two people stood on them.

base.name: Interior
base.description: Inside the building. The walls, the floor, the ceiling — all solid. All reliable. All taken for granted.

geological_event+natural_disaster.name: City Overlook
geological_event+natural_disaster.description: A stone wall looking down over the city grid. The low vibration has deepened into a subterranean roar, like a fleet of freight trains rushing beneath the earth.

examine_details.city_grid: The city looks completely normal from up here. Millions of people going about their day, totally unaware of the monstrous energy building beneath them.
examine_details.stone_wall: The mortar between the stones is turning to dust and spilling out as the vibrations intensify.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: Parking Structure — Stairwell
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: A concrete stairwell echoing with the roar of the rain and distant sirens. Water is cascading down the steps from the open roof above.
urban_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
You push through the heavy fire doors into the stairwell. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is already climbing, soaked to the bone, looking back at you with wild eyes.

Outside, you hear a loud, metallic screech as the street's electrical transformer blows.

examine_details.concrete_stairs: Solid concrete. Slippery, but a lot safer than the rising floodwaters below.
examine_details.cascading_water: Rainwater is pouring down the center gap of the stairwell like an indoor waterfall.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: Bridge West Side — Traffic Stop
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: The west end of the bridge. Traffic has stopped — a fender bender up ahead. Horns blare. The bridge vibrates with the weight of stationary vehicles. You can feel a rhythmic swaying that wasn't there before.

examine_details.stopped_traffic: Vehicles packed bumper to bumper. Nobody can move. The school bus is right in the middle of the span.
examine_details.bridge_sway: The bridge is swaying. Not from wind — from weight. The rhythm is wrong. It's getting worse.
examine_details.cracking_sound: A deep, structural groan. Like the sound a frozen lake makes before it gives way. But this isn't ice. This is steel and concrete.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: Parking Structure — Stairwell
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: A concrete stairwell echoing with the sound of car alarms going off outside. The entire building shudders periodically.
urban_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
You push through the heavy fire doors into the stairwell. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is already climbing, looking down at you with terrified eyes.

Outside, you hear a loud, subterranean SNAP.

examine_details.concrete_stairs: Solid concrete. They feel a lot safer than the asphalt outside.
examine_details.vibrating_handrail: The metal railing is humming like a tuning fork. Something massive is shifting beneath the city.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: First Floor Hallway
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A long, dimly lit corridor. The doors to the apartments are all shut. The floor is slanting noticeably to the left.

examine_details.slanting_floor: If you put a marble down here, it would roll into the wall with frightening speed.
examine_details.door_frames: None of the doors sit square in their frames anymore. The building is actively settling.

base.name: Upper Level
base.description: Higher ground. A view of what's below. A view of what's coming.
base.first_entry_text: From up here, you can see everything.

geological_event+natural_disaster.name: Hilltop Park — Open Ground
geological_event+natural_disaster.description: You sprinted to the center of the park, away from the trees, away from the power lines.
geological_event+natural_disaster.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you by the collar and made you sprint toward the center of the park, screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the bones of the city snapping.'[/color] You reached the grass just as the air seemed to [color=#aaaaaa]curdle[/color] with a low-frequency hum.

The P-wave hit first—a sudden, [color=#FF3131]violent jolt[/color] that traveled up through your marrow and knocked you to your knees. Then, the S-wave arrived, and the world became [color=#55aaff]liquid[/color]. The solid earth began to roll in nauseating, five-foot swells like the surface of a [color=#55aaff]storm-tossed ocean[/color].

You watched as the brick facade of the library across the street began to [color=#aaaaaa]peel away[/color] from its steel skeleton. It didn't just fall; the masonry [color=#FF3131]disintegrated[/color] into a spray of red dust before the weight of the roof hammered the cars below into [color=#aaaaaa]scrap metal[/color]. The sound is a [color=#FF3131]deafening, planetary grinding[/color] mixed with the [color=#aaaaaa]shriek of unzipping rebar[/color] and the distance-muffled screams of the trapped.

The earth [color=#FF3131]rips open[/color] along the park path—a jagged, [color=#aaaaaa]hungry fissure[/color] that unzips the soil with a sound like a thousand dry branches breaking at once. It expands rapidly toward your feet, the abyss showing only [color=#55aaff]churning mud and broken pipes[/color], before it finally halts.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] You stand in the silence of the settling dust, looking at the [color=#FF3131]pulverized horizon[/color]. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the planet's teeth shift before the first stone moved. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]swallowed[/color] by that street.

examine_details.fissure: A jagged crack in the earth, easily three feet wide and growing.
examine_details.collapsing_library: A cloud of brick dust obscures the street. The aftershocks are already starting.

structural_collapse.name: East Bank Overlook
structural_collapse.description: You made it off the bridge. You're standing on the east bank, looking back at the span. The swaying is visible now — a slow pendulum motion that bridges are not supposed to make.
structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] physically pulled you off the bridge.

You turn around when you hear it — the first sounds of metal.

The centre span drops first. Not a gradual failure — a sudden, sickening lurch downward, like a trapdoor releasing. Cables snap like guitar strings, each one a separate rifle crack.

The sound is a screeching barrage of metal on asphalt and then metal on water and then silence, which is worse.

The bridge folds in half. Vehicles slide into the gap. A school bus. A delivery truck. A car with its hazards on that had been stopped in traffic.

The river swallows everything.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was right.

examine_details.collapsed_span: The centre span of the {city_name} Memorial Bridge hangs vertically, submerged to its midpoint. Suspension cables trail in the current like dead vines. The span is still moving — swaying in the current.
examine_details.river_debris: Vehicles are visible just below the surface. One pair of taillights is still on, blinking red in the murk. Emergency sirens layer over each other from both banks.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: {city_name} Business District — Parking Structure Roof
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: The open roof of the parking structure adjacent to the business district. You came up here on your lunch break. You weren't hungry.
urban_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you by the collar, their breath smelling of [color=#FF3131]adrenaline and iron[/color], and dragged you up the concrete stairs of the parking structure. They wouldn't let you stay on the street—they kept screaming about [color=#aaaaaa]'the ground turning into a mouth.'[/color]

The section of pavement you were standing on thirty seconds ago [color=#FF3131]no longer exists[/color].

It didn't crack or settle with a warning. It [color=#aaaaaa]inverted[/color]—the center simply vanished into a black void, and the asphalt edges curled downward like [color=#FF3131]melting wax[/color]. There was no explosion, only the [color=#aaaaaa]sickening, wet slurp[/color] of a thousand tons of earth being swallowed at once.

The sinkhole is a [color=#FF3131]growing, hungry circle[/color]. From this height, you can see the cause: a massive, [color=#55aaff]corroded water main[/color] that has been scouring the soil into a slurry for years. The road is a hollow shell, and the weight of the world is finally breaking it. 

A food cart. Two parked cars. A bus stop shelter. [color=#FF3131]A person reaching for a door handle[/color]. 

They don't fall; they are [color=#FF3131]erased[/color]. Plunged into a subterranean grave of [color=#55aaff]churning mud and broken concrete[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the vibration in the soles of their feet before the first pebble dropped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]suffocated[/color] in that dark.

examine_details.sinkhole_panorama: The sinkhole is approximately eighty feet across and still widening at its edges. The depth is unclear — the bottom isn't visible. Utility lines are dangling into it from all sides.
examine_details.subsidence_crack: A crack has propagated from the sinkhole along the street toward the parking structure you're standing on. It's about four inches wide. It wasn't there when you came up here.

construction_failure.name: Opposite Pavement — Doorway
construction_failure.description: The doorway of the corner shop across the street. You ducked in here. The building opposite — your building — has been making a sound for the last thirty seconds.
construction_failure.first_entry_text:
The sound was [color=#aaaaaa]subterranean[/color]. A deep, rhythmic groaning from the foundation was transmitted through the pavement and into the marrow of your bones. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] didn't just warn you; they [color=#FF3131]seized you by the throat[/color] and dragged you across the asphalt, shoving you into a brick doorway just as the air began to [color=#aaaaaa]vibrate[/color].

The building doesn't collapse dramatically; it collapses [color=#aaaaaa]practically[/color]. You watch with a hollow chest as the floors [color=#FF3131]pancake in sequence[/color], a mechanical heartbeat of stone-on-stone. Each level adds its mass to the next, the whole structure [color=#FF3131]compressing downward[/color] with a slow, deliberate gravity that [color=#aaaaaa]unzips the support columns[/color] like dry brittle.

The air trapped inside the lobby has nowhere to go. As the weight descends, the ground floor windows [color=#FF3131]detonate outward[/color] from the sheer atmospheric pressure. A [color=#aaaaaa]hurricane of jagged glass[/color] shrieks across the street, moving with the velocity of shrapnel. One jagged shard—eight inches of razor-sharp plate—slams into the door frame [color=#FF3131]inches from your temple[/color], vibrating with a high, singing note.

A [color=#aaaaaa]silt-heavy dust cloud[/color] the size of a city block exhales from the ruins, smelling of [color=#aaaaaa]pulverized concrete and old insulation[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the concrete fatigue before the first rebar snapped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]compacted[/color] between those floors.

examine_details.rubble_mound: The building is now approximately twelve feet tall where it was six stories. A stairwell column is still standing — the rest is a uniform grey mound. You can hear something from inside it. Something alive.
examine_details.glass_scatter: Glass covers the entire pavement on both sides of the street. Some pieces are still falling from the floors that haven't fully settled.

natural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Rooftop Terrace — Overlook
natural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: The rooftop terrace of your highrise building. From up here, you have a terrifying, unobstructed view of the ruined city grid below.
natural_disaster+structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
You chased [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] up the emergency stairwell to the roof, screaming for them to make sense, as they burst through the terrace doors rasping about [color=#FF3131]'concrete rolling like waves'[/color]

Car alarms triggered all at once—a frantic, [color=#aaaaaa]unison of terror[/color] echoing off the downtown highrises. Then came the P-wave. It was a rolling, almost [color=#55aaff]nauseating sensation[/color], the entire skyscraper swaying under your feet like a slow boat in deep swells. You looked down; the coffee in your hand was rippling in perfect, [color=#aaaaaa]concentric circles[/color] of doom.

The S-wave hit four seconds later, and the metro grid became a [color=#FF3131]meat-grinder[/color].

The historic masonry building across the avenue—six floors of heavy brick and mortar—didn't just crumble. In the distance, the suspension bridge violently whipped like a jump rope as the city's foundation [color=#aaaaaa]liquefied[/color]. The brick building tilted sideways with the slow, ponderous grace of a dying giant. You watched from above as the structural seams [color=#FF3131]erupted in a spray of red dust[/color] before the entire mass slammed into the asphalt, the impact [color=#FF3131]shattering[/color] the windows of every car on the block.

The highrise is still groaning. The earth has been moving for thirty seconds, a [color=#aaaaaa]low, rhythmic growl[/color] that sounds like the planet's own teeth grinding together. It doesn't stop. It just [color=#FF3131]keeps moving[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the fault line snap before the first foundation shifted. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]pulverized[/color] down there under those floors of brick.

examine_details.ruined_avenue: Half the buildings below are severely damaged, their facades sheared off to reveal the ruined apartments inside. Gas is burning somewhere two streets over. You can hear the fire roaring above the distant sirens.
examine_details.fractured_parapet: The concrete safety wall at the edge of the roof has cracked straight through. The fissure is four inches wide, a terrifying reminder of the sheer stress this skyscraper just endured.

drowning_water+transportation_water.name: Lakeshore Promenade — North Pier
drowning_water+transportation_water.description: The north pier of the promenade. You watched the Queen Isabella pull away from here. You almost didn't get off in time.
drowning_water+transportation_water.first_entry_text:
You were the last one down the gangway, shoved into the air by a panicked, [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] whose hands were [color=#FF3131]trembling with a violent, animal heat[/color]. The deckhand was already unhooking the chains when your boots hit the pier.

You stood on the concrete and watched the [color=#aaaaaa]Queen Isabella[/color] groan as she made her turn into the black water. The lights were a [color=#FF3131]blinding, festive gold[/color] across all five decks, casting long, shimmering needles onto the lake.

The list started with a [color=#aaaaaa]low, structural moan[/color]—a degree, then two. It looked almost graceful, until the [color=#aaaaaa]rattle of sliding furniture[/color] began to echo across the water. Through the portholes, you see tiny glints of silver and porcelain [color=#FF3131]tumbling into the abyss[/color] as the dining room floors become walls.

By fifteen degrees, the [color=#FF3131]screaming[/color] reached the shore—a thin, collective wail that was abruptly drowned out by the [color=#aaaaaa]shriek of unzipping rivets[/color]. 

The [color=#aaaaaa]Queen Isabella[/color] rolled past the point of recovery and simply [color=#FF3131]surrendered[/color]. The massive funnel hit the water first with a [color=#FF3131]hiss of escaping steam and boiling blood[/color], before the entire hull came over on top of the lifeboats, [color=#FF3131]snuffing out the lights[/color] and the voices in one final, [color=#aaaaaa]heavy gulp[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the center of gravity shift before the first rope was even loosed. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]sealed in that steel tomb[/color].

examine_details.capsized_hull: The hull of the Queen Isabella is visible above the waterline — red anti-fouling paint against black water. It's still moving, slowly rotating as it settles.
examine_details.flotation_debris: The lake surface between the pier and the wreck is covered in life rings, deck chairs, luggage, and people. Rescue boats are launching from the south pier.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: Parking Structure — Level Three
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: The open third level of the parking structure. You came up here to get away from the street. The rain hit like a wall five minutes ago and hasn't stopped.
urban_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
The storm drain on the corner of 4th and Main failed with a [color=#aaaaaa]sickening, subterranean vomit[/color]. You watched from the sidewalk as the street inverted—the water erupting [color=#55aaff]upward[/color] in a geyser of silt and sewage instead of draining away.

You scrambled up the concrete stairs of the parking structure, following the [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] as they clawed their way toward the upper decks. You felt like a fool—your car was parked on level one—but the [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] wouldn't stop screaming about [color=#FF3131]a flash flood that was coming.[/color]

In minutes, the downtown corridor is transformed into a [color=#55aaff]roaring, debris-choked canyon[/color]. It isn't just a flood; it’s a high-velocity river with a [color=#FF3131]killing current[/color] fast enough to tumble parked SUVs like [color=#aaaaaa]scrap metal[/color]. The water hits a blown transformer box on the corner. The blue flash arcs across the surface of the water, [color=#3399ff]electrocuting[/color] everyone still trying to swim.

The traffic signals are still cycling with [color=#aaaaaa]blind, robotic indifference[/color]. Green. Yellow. Red. Reflecting off a surface that is now [color=#FF3131]thick with gasoline and wreckage[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the pressure in the pipes before the first manhole cover blew. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]drowned and crushed[/color] in that lower deck.

examine_details.flood_street_view: The entire block is underwater. The water line is at the top of the parking meters — about four feet. It's still rising. The current is carrying debris: bins, tyres, a push-bike.
examine_details.drainage_failure: You can see where the storm drain gave out — a geyser of brown water is still shooting up from the grate like a fountain. The city's drainage system has reversed.

visionary_player+geological_event+natura.first_entry_text:
You were sprinting before the P-wave arrived.

The low-frequency hum — [color=#aaaaaa]subsonic, felt more than heard[/color] — you'd been waiting for it since you woke up, knowing the frequency, knowing what followed.

You seized people by the collar and ran them toward the center of the park. You screamed about [color=#FF3131]'the bones of the city snapping'[/color] because that's what it looks like in the images: the grid of streets flexing like a living thing.

The ground moves. [color=#FF3131]Exactly as you saw.[/color]

visionary_player+structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
You pulled people off the bridge.

Physically. By their jackets, their arms, whatever you could reach. You'd seen the centre span drop — that specific, sickening lurch downward, [color=#FF3131]the cables snapping like guitar strings[/color], each one a separate sound — and you knew the precise moment it would start.

You cleared the bridge. People were angry. You stayed angry back.

The first sounds of metal come from above. Then the centre span drops, [color=#FF3131]exactly on schedule.[/color]

visionary_player+urban_disaster+geologic.first_entry_text:
You were already moving people up the parking structure stairs
before you fully understood what you were doing.

The [color=#FF3131]smell of adrenaline and iron[/color] had been in your mouth since morning, the taste of something about to break, and you couldn't stay on the street knowing what the ground was going to do.

You screamed about [color=#aaaaaa]'the ground turning into liquid.'[/color] People backed away from you. You kept moving.

From the upper level, [color=#FF3131]the street does exactly that.[/color]

visionary_player+construction_failure.first_entry_text:
The sound was [color=#aaaaaa]subterranean.[/color]

A deep, rhythmic groaning transmitted through the pavement and into your bones. You'd felt it before — in the images — and you knew exactly how long between this vibration and the failure of the foundation.

You seized people by the throat and dragged them across the street. Not metaphorically. You used your hands.

The scaffolding comes down [color=#FF3131]right where you'd been standing.[/color] The dust cloud reaches you up here.

visionary_player+natural_disaster+struct.first_entry_text:
You burst through the terrace doors first.

You'd been on the emergency stairwell for thirty seconds already, hauling people by the arm, when the [color=#FF3131]car alarms triggered all at once[/color] — that frantic, unison chorus that means the ground is moving.

You'd rasped about [color=#FF3131]'concrete rolling like waves'[/color] until the words stopped making sense even to you. But you kept climbing.

From the roof, the building below you [color=#FF3131]does exactly that.[/color]

visionary_player+drowning_water+transpor.first_entry_text:
You shoved people down the gangway.

Your hands were [color=#FF3131]trembling with a violent, animal heat[/color] — not fear, exactly, but the physical response to knowing something that your body couldn't contain and your mouth couldn't explain.

You got everyone off the ship before you got off yourself. The deckhand was already unhooking the chains when your boots hit the pier.

You stood on the concrete and watched the ship you'd evacuated [color=#FF3131]do what you already knew it was going to do.[/color]

visionary_player+urban_disaster+flood_re.first_entry_text:
You were already on the concrete barrier when the drain failed.

You'd watched the [color=#aaaaaa]storm drain on the corner of 4th and Main[/color] in the images all morning — the street inverting, water erupting upward — and you'd spent the last twenty minutes getting people to higher ground using every excuse you had.

The drain vomits upward exactly as you saw it would. From up here, [color=#55aaff]the water spreads[/color] in a perfect circle around [color=#FF3131]exactly the spot you cleared.[/color]

outdoor._description: Nature path: trailhead/arrival → open area → viewpoint/shelter → high ground. Covers wildfires, floods, earthquakes, typhoons, ski lifts, sinkholes, dam breaks.

base.name: Arrival Point
base.description: The starting point. A parking lot, a trailhead, a dock. The edge of civilization.
base.first_entry_text:
You're outside. The air is fresh. Nothing bad happens outdoors. Right?

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started screaming.

car_radio.name: Car Radio
car_radio.description: A battered car nearby is blaring their radio for all to hear.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
Open sky. Doesn't feel safe.

In the vision it started from above — or the ground — you're still not entirely sure of the geometry. You just know that this exact spot, with this many people, [color=ff4444]is exactly wrong.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The outdoor area. People are spread out, relaxed, enjoying the weather. You know what the weather is about to do.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Parking Area
natural_disaster.description: A gravel parking lot at the McKinley scenic overlook. A few cars. A ranger station with a CLOSED sign. The sky has an odd color — yellow-grey, like a bruise.
natural_disaster.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] ripped you out of the car, screaming something about the sky.

The sky looks wrong. The air tastes metallic. The birds are gone — completely silent.

examine_details.sky_color: The sky is the color of a old bruise — yellow, grey, with veins of green. You've never seen a sky like this.
examine_details.silent_trees: The forest is absolutely silent. No birds. No insects. No wind in the leaves. It's like nature is holding its breath.
examine_details.weather_station: A small automated weather station. The wind speed dial is pinned. The barometer is dropping so fast you can watch the needle move.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: McKinley National Forest — Trailhead
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: A dusty gravel parking area at the edge of the dense pine forest. The air is uncomfortably hot and completely still. It smells intensely of baked pine needles and dry dirt.
natural_disaster+fire_related.first_entry_text:
You stepped out of your car and immediately started sweating. It’s unseasonably hot today. 

[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is leaning against the trailhead map, panting, looking paranoid at the tree line. 'It smells like a struck match,' they mutter.

examine_details.dry_brush: The undergrowth is yellow and brittle. It hasn't rained in a month. If you stepped on it, it would crunch like glass.
examine_details.distant_haze: There is a faint, yellowish haze hanging over the canopy in the east. Probably just smog.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: McKinley Beach — Parking Lot
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: An empty, sand-blown asphalt lot facing the ocean. The sky is a bruised, unnatural purple. The wind is already strong enough to make walking difficult.
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.first_entry_text:
You drove out to watch the storm roll in. A lot of locals do it. But as soon as you parked, [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] started hyperventilating, begging you to put the car in reverse.

The wind howls across the asphalt, carrying sharp, stinging grains of sand.

examine_details.dark_horizon: The clouds offshore are a solid, terrifying wall of black. There is no horizon line—just a unified front of violence moving toward the coast.
examine_details.ocean_roar: The surf sounds different today. It's a continuous, low-frequency booming that vibrates the car windows.

natural_disaster+geological_event.name: Hilltop Park — Entrance
natural_disaster+geological_event.description: The paved entrance to the sprawling city park. Strangely, every dog in the vicinity is barking frantically, pulling at their leashes.
natural_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
You arrived at the park to clear your head. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] is already here, pacing wildly and claiming the ground is 'screaming'.

A massive flock of birds suddenly erupts from the trees in unison, fleeing the area.

examine_details.fleeing_birds: Hundreds of them, flying erratically. Animals always know before we do.
examine_details.panicked_dogs: A dog walker is struggling to hold onto three golden retrievers that are desperately trying to drag him back toward the street.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: {city_name} Dam — Visitor Parking
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: A gravel parking lot overlooking the massive concrete wall of the dam. The sky looks bruised, and the air is heavy.
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.first_entry_text: [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] dragged you out of the car, eyes wide, pointing at the concrete face of the dam...

examine_details.dam_wall: A colossal slab of concrete holding back millions of tons of water. You notice a damp patch near the base that wasn't there before.
examine_details.sky_color: The sky is the color of an old bruise...
examine_details.weather_station: The barometer is dropping so fast you can watch the needle move.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: McKinley Peak — Base Lodge
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The base lodge of McKinley Peak ski resort. Skiers and boarders mill about in colorful gear. The lift line stretches across the snow. Gondolas swing overhead on cables.
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
Perfect conditions — fresh powder, blue sky, light wind. The mountain is packed.

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]threw their skis down in the lift line and started shouting that the cables were going to snap. People are staring.

examine_details.lift_line: A queue of skiers shuffles forward. Kids, families, a few show-offs doing tricks in line. Nobody's worried.
examine_details.gondola_cables: Thick steel cables run from the base station up the mountain, disappearing into the trees. The gondolas sway gently. One of them makes a grinding sound as it passes the first tower.
examine_details.conditions_board: CONDITIONS: POWDER — 8" NEW. WIND: 15 MPH GUSTING TO 35. AVALANCHE RISK: MODERATE. Someone taped a note under it: 'Lift 3 cable tension check OVERDUE — see maintenance.'

base.name: Open Area
base.description: A wide open space. Exposed. Nowhere to hide if things go wrong.

visionary_player.first_entry_text:
The largest open area. The most people. The furthest from any cover.

This is where you need to thin the crowd. Every person you convince to leave is a person not standing here when it happens.

[color=ff4444]Keep moving.[/color]
visionary_player.description: The central outdoor area. Exposed. You feel the wrongness of this place the way some people feel rain coming.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Trail
natural_disaster.description: A dirt trail through sparse trees. The overlook point is ahead. The ground feels unstable — soft, almost spongy. Cracks have appeared in the trail surface.

examine_details.ground_cracks: Fresh cracks in the packed dirt. Some are an inch wide. They weren't here last week — you can tell because the exposed earth is dark and moist.
examine_details.leaning_trees: Several trees are tilted at odd angles. Their roots are partially exposed. The ground shifted under them.
examine_details.distant_rumble: A low rumble from somewhere underground. Not thunder. Deeper. Constant.

natural_disaster+geological_event.name: Hilltop Park — Great Lawn
natural_disaster+geological_event.description: A wide expanse of grass. The air is dead still, but you can feel a distinct, high-frequency vibration buzzing through the soil.

examine_details.vibrating_ground: It tickles the soles of your feet. It's not a localized rumble; it feels like the entire tectonic plate is humming.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: River Trail
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: A dirt trail running parallel to the river. The water is brown and churning violently, already cresting the muddy banks. An emergency siren wails continuously from the dam upstream.

examine_details.swollen_river: The water level has risen two feet in the last ten minutes. It's moving with a terrifying, muscular speed.
examine_details.siren_noise: A mechanical, sustained wail echoing down the valley. It's an evacuation alarm.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Lift Loading Zone
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The loading zone for Lift 3. Skiers shuffle forward, grabbing chairs as they swing around. An attendant waves people into position. The chairs climb steeply into the trees.

examine_details.lift_chairs: Two-person chairs, swinging around the bullwheel. They look sturdy enough. But one of them has a safety bar that doesn't latch.
examine_details.loading_platform: A rubber mat worn smooth from boots. The attendant looks bored. He hasn't checked the cable monitor in at least an hour.
examine_details.cable_tension_monitor: A small screen showing cable tension readings. The left column reads NOMINAL. The right column reads ALERT — TENSION VARIANCE 12%. Normal variance is under 3%.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: Pine Ridge Trail
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: A narrow dirt path cutting through dense, old-growth pines. The heat is oppressive. Something feels deeply wrong with the ecosystem here today.

examine_details.fleeing_wildlife: A stag bursts from the brush, entirely ignoring you, and sprints west. The animals aren't just moving; they are running for their lives.
examine_details.falling_ash: You hold out your hand. A single, grey flake of ash lands on your palm. Then another. It's starting to 'snow'.
examine_details.oppressive_heat: The temperature has spiked at least ten degrees in the last five minutes.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: Beachfront Boardwalk
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: A wide wooden boardwalk separating the beach from the coastal shops. The wind is shrieking through the railings. The surf is already cresting the seawall.

examine_details.crashing_surf: The waves are chaotic, completely out of rhythm. Brown foam is spraying twenty feet into the air as they hammer the concrete.
examine_details.boarded_windows: Every shop is covered in fresh plywood. Some idiot sprayed 'BRING IT ON' across the bait shop's boards.
examine_details.whipping_flags: The warning flags on the lifeguard stand are snapping so hard the fabric is tearing.

base.name: Shelter
base.description: A structure offering some protection from the elements.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Ranger Cabin
natural_disaster.description: A small ranger cabin, locked and boarded. A radio antenna on the roof sways in wind that wasn't there five minutes ago. An emergency supply box is mounted on the exterior wall.

examine_details.radio_antenna: The antenna whips back and forth. The wind is picking up fast — and it's coming from the wrong direction.
examine_details.emergency_map: A faded evacuation map under cracked plexiglass. EVACUATION ROUTE: FOLLOW TRAIL SOUTH TO PARKING AREA. IN CASE OF EARTHQUAKE: MOVE TO OPEN GROUND.
examine_details.seismograph_printout: A curl of paper from a portable seismograph, still printing. The line is no longer flat. It's spiking. Rhythmically. Getting bigger.

natural_disaster+geological_event.name: Park Gazebo
natural_disaster+geological_event.description: A sturdy wooden gazebo. The timber frame is groaning rhythmically, as if the foundation it sits on is subtly twisting.

examine_details.creaking_timbers: The wood is flexing. The gazebo isn't moving, but the earth beneath its footings is shifting in opposite directions.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: Riverside Picnic Pavilion
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: A covered pavilion near the water. The roar of the swollen river almost drowns out the blaring warning sirens. The ground shakes with the force of the water pressing against the dam.

examine_details.abandoned_picnic: Half-eaten sandwiches and a spilled cooler. Whoever was here heard the siren and ran.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Ski Patrol Hut
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: A small red hut with a white cross. Ski patrol. A stretcher leans against the wall. A radio crackles inside with chatter about 'Lift 3 anomalies.'

examine_details.patrol_radio: 'Control, this is Patrol 7. We're getting reports of unusual vibration on Lift 3. Passengers are complaining. Over.' Static. No response.
examine_details.incident_log: A clipboard. The last three entries are all about Lift 3: 'Cable tension variance.' 'Grinding sound at Tower 4.' 'Gondola #17 safety latch failure.' All from today.
examine_details.mountain_map: A topographic map of McKinley Peak. Someone has drawn a red circle around Tower 4 of Lift 3 and written 'CHECK IMMEDIATELY' in block letters.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: Hiker's Lean-To
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: A three-sided timber shelter meant for unexpected rain. Right now, it's just a wooden box trapping the heat. The air is thick with the smell of woodsmoke.

examine_details.thickening_smoke: The haze has become a fog. The sun is a dull, blood-red disc filtering through the canopy.
examine_details.wind_shift: The dead, still air suddenly whips up into a fierce gale. But it's not cooling anything down—it feels like standing in front of an open oven door.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: Concrete Lifeguard Station
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: A heavy, reinforced concrete bunker used by the beach patrol. You ducked inside to escape the stinging rain.

examine_details.vibrating_walls: The concrete is thick, but every time a wave hits the seawall outside, the entire bunker shudders.
examine_details.pressure_drop: Your ears pop painfully. The barometric pressure is bottoming out at a terrifying speed.

base.name: Viewpoint
base.description: The highest accessible point. A panoramic view of everything below.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Cliff Edge
natural_disaster.description: The scenic overlook. A waist-high railing. Below, the valley spreads out — {city_name}, the river, the downtown core. The ground vibrates under your feet.

examine_details.valley_view: {city_name} sprawls below. From here it looks peaceful. Orderly. Fragile.
examine_details.vibrating_ground: The ground is vibrating. Not shaking — vibrating. A constant, low-frequency hum. The railing buzzes against your palms.
examine_details.railing: A metal safety railing. The bolts anchoring it to the rock are loose. You can wiggle them with your fingers.

natural_disaster+geological_event.name: City Overlook
natural_disaster+geological_event.description: A stone wall looking down over the city grid. The low vibration has deepened into a subterranean roar, like a fleet of freight trains rushing beneath the earth.

examine_details.city_grid: The city looks completely normal from up here. Millions of people going about their day, totally unaware of the monstrous energy building beneath them.
examine_details.stone_wall: The mortar between the stones is turning to dust and spilling out as the vibrations intensify.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: Dam Observation Deck
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: A steel platform looking directly at the face of the massive concrete dam. A terrifying network of spiderweb cracks is visibly spreading across the center span, weeping pressurized water.

examine_details.cracked_dam: Millions of gallons of water are pressing against failing concrete. The cracks are actively widening as you watch, shooting high-pressure jets of water into the air.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Mid-Station Platform
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The mid-station where Lift 3 passes through a support structure. From here you can see the cable running up to the summit and back down to the base. Gondolas full of skiers slide past.

examine_details.cable_closeup: The cable passes inches from the platform. You can hear it — a low singing sound, like a guitar string under too much tension. The pitch is rising.
examine_details.tower_four: Tower 4 is visible up the slope. Something is wrong with it — the crossarm is tilted. The cable is riding on the edge of the sheave, not in the groove.
examine_details.summit_view: The summit of {city_name} Peak. Beautiful. Dozens of people are up there, waiting for the lift to bring them down.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: Eagle Rock Overlook
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: A granite outcropping offering a panoramic view of the valley below. You can see exactly where the smoke is coming from.

examine_details.valley_inferno: A massive, uncontrolled crown fire is consuming the valley floor. It's moving with terrifying speed, jumping hundreds of yards at a time.
examine_details.wind_direction: The fierce gale is blowing directly up the slope. The fire isn't just moving; the wind is driving it straight toward your position.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: Pier Entrance
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: The elevated entrance to the town's wooden pier. The pier itself is already taking catastrophic damage from the surf.

examine_details.shattering_pier: Massive pilings are snapping like matchsticks. The ocean is rapidly dismantling the structure.
examine_details.the_surge: The water isn't receding between waves anymore. A solid, black wall of ocean is simply rising, preparing to swallow the shoreline.

base.name: High Ground
base.description: The safest point. The place you end up when everything below falls apart.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Treeline Clearing
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: A clearing in the trees above the mid-station. You hiked up instead of taking the lift. You're not sure why you believed them.
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You were still wondering if you fully believed [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] when they suddenly bolted from the lift line and scrambled up the adjacent hiking trail. You followed them.

[color=#5eafd2]Then, the main cable makes a sound.[/color]

Not the usual steady, mechanical hum. It's a harmonic —a high, singing vibration, rising in pitch until it sets your teeth on edge.

With a sound like a cannon shot, [color=#BC13FE]Tower 4's massive steel crossarm shears clean off[/color].

The tension releases in a violent whip. The cable lashes out, shearing through fifty-foot pines on both sides of the line. The gondolas plummet, [color=#5eafd2]but they don't hit the snow[/color]—they pendulum wildly out over the mountain pass road below, right into the path of a transit bus that had left from the lodge.

A giant capsule of steel and glass slams into it broadside - [color=#5eafd2]the impact yanking the massive lift cable taut[/color]. Tires skidding desperately on the black ice, the bus is violently dragged off course. It tips and rolls heavily down the steep grade, [color=#BC13FE]wrapping itself in the snapped cable like a horrific spool[/color].

The tumbling bus acts as a massive anchor, dragging the entire lift system down with it. [color=#5eafd2]Towers buckle in a catastrophic domino effect.[/color] More gondolas are ripped from the sky, smashing into the lodge as the cable begins [color=#BC13FE]sawing through the patio supports[/color] as the crowd above looks on in absolute, frozen horror.

The mountain echoes with a chorus of tearing metal and screaming steel.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was actually right.

examine_details.fallen_gondolas: Gondolas lie scattered down the slope. Some are crumpled against the trees. Others are half-buried in the snow. You can count eight from here. The lift was carrying forty.
examine_details.snapped_cable: The steel cable lies across the snow in a massive arc, still under tension where it's caught on Tower 3. The trees it cut through are still falling.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: Coastal Bluff — Storm Shelter Ruins
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: The concrete shelter at the top of the coastal bluff. Its roof is gone — took by a previous storm years ago. The walls are still standing.
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.first_entry_text:
The forecast predicted a Category 2. It slammed into the coast as a [color=#FF3131]screaming Category 4[/color].

You were on the boardwalk when the first band hit, and the air turned into a [color=#aaaaaa]shrieking, solid weight[/color]. The wind was already wrong—not gusting, but a sustained, [color=#FF3131]predatory pressure[/color] that leaned against your ribs until you couldn't breathe. 

[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you, their fingers bruising your arm, and dragged you up the bluff path toward the old shelter, screaming about the [color=#aaaaaa]'ocean coming to reclaim its floor.'[/color]

From the ridge, you see the full, [color=#FF3131]apocalyptic sweep[/color] of the coastline. The storm surge is coming in now—it isn't waves; it’s a [color=#55aaff]dark, silt-choked wall of the abyss[/color]. It is a continuous, rising elevation of the sea level, advancing with a [color=#aaaaaa]low, rhythmic thunder[/color]. The boardwalk doesn't just flood; it is [color=#FF3131]pulverized and swallowed[/color] in thirty seconds. The parking lot vanishes in two minutes.

Then, the sound of [color=#aaaaaa]shearing bolts[/color]. The buildings that aren't anchored to the bedrock begin to drift, rotating like toy boats before the currents [color=#FF3131]hammer them into toothpicks[/color] against the bluff.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the barometric pressure drop in their marrow. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]drowned and shredded[/color] in that surf.

examine_details.surge_panorama: The coastline below is no longer coastline. It's sea. The storm surge has advanced three blocks inland. Buildings are standing in water to their second floors.
examine_details.shelter_wall: The windward wall of the shelter is flexing — you can feel it vibrating under your hands. The wind speed up here is doing things you didn't think wind could do.

geological_event+urban_disaster.name: {city_name} Business District — Parking Structure Roof
geological_event+urban_disaster.description: The open roof of the parking structure adjacent to the business district. You came up here on your lunch break. You weren't hungry.
geological_event+urban_disaster.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you by the collar, their breath smelling of [color=#FF3131]adrenaline and iron[/color], and dragged you up the concrete stairs of the parking structure. They wouldn't let you stay on the street—they kept screaming about [color=#aaaaaa]'the ground turning into a mouth.'[/color]

The section of pavement you were standing on thirty seconds ago [color=#FF3131]no longer exists[/color].

It didn't crack or settle with a warning. It [color=#aaaaaa]inverted[/color]—the center simply vanished into a black void, and the asphalt edges curled downward like [color=#FF3131]melting wax[/color]. There was no explosion, only the [color=#aaaaaa]sickening, wet slurp[/color] of a thousand tons of earth being swallowed at once.

The sinkhole is a [color=#FF3131]growing, hungry circle[/color]. From this height, you can see the cause: a massive, [color=#55aaff]corroded water main[/color] that has been scouring the soil into a slurry for years. The road is a hollow shell, and the weight of the world is finally breaking it. 

A food cart. Two parked cars. A bus stop shelter. [color=#FF3131]A person reaching for a door handle[/color]. 

They don't fall; they are [color=#FF3131]erased[/color]. Plunged into a subterranean grave of [color=#55aaff]churning mud and broken concrete[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the vibration in the soles of their feet before the first pebble dropped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]suffocated[/color] in that dark.

examine_details.sinkhole_panorama: The sinkhole is approximately eighty feet across and still widening at its edges. The depth is unclear — the bottom isn't visible. Utility lines are dangling into it from all sides.
examine_details.subsidence_crack: A crack has propagated from the sinkhole along the street toward the parking structure you're standing on. It's about four inches wide. It wasn't there when you came up here.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: {city_name} National Forest — Fire Road Crest
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: The crest of the fire road, above the smoke line. You can breathe up here. Barely.
natural_disaster+fire_related.first_entry_text:
[color=#aaaaaa]The smoke descended the ridge like a heavy, suffocating fog.[/color] It wasn't drifting; it was rolling in [color=#FF3131]thick, oily waves[/color] that tasted of [color=#FF3131]charred pine and panic[/color].

You’d been hiking the loop trail when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] erupted from the undergrowth—not running, but [color=#FF3131]sprinting[/color] with a frantic, animal energy, their eyes streaming and [color=#FF3131]bloodshot from the heat[/color]. They seized your pack and [color=#FF3131]hauled you up the vertical slope[/color] toward the fire road, screaming that the car park was already a [color=#FF3131]chimney[/color].

The crown fire crested the ridge two minutes later. It was a [color=#FF3131]sixty-foot wall of incandescent fury[/color] leaping through the canopy at twenty miles an hour. The sound was a [color=#aaaaaa]physical blow[/color]—not the crackle of a campfire, but a [color=#FF3131]deafening, low-frequency roar[/color] like a jet engine anchored to the earth, [color=#aaaaaa]shredding the oxygen[/color] out of the air.

You watch as the heat front arrives before the flames, the trees in the valley below [color=#FF3131]spontaneously combusting[/color] in a flash of blinding light. Your car—your only way out—is down there, melting into a [color=#aaaaaa]puddle of alloy and glass[/color].
